Set-Content -Path .env.production -Value "DEBUG=False"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# COPY - Duplicating files and directories
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Copy-Item settings.py settings.py.backup     # Simple copy
Copy-Item -Path settings.py -Destination backup\ -Confirm  # Interactive

# Copy directory recursively (like cp -r)
Copy-Item -Path templates -Destination templates_backup -Recurse

# Copy multiple files
Copy-Item -Path *.py -Destination scripts\
Copy-Item models.py, views.py, urls.py -Destination app_backup\

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# MOVE / RENAME
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Move-Item old.py new.py                       # Rename
Rename-Item old.py -NewName new.py            # Explicit rename

Move-Item deploy.ps1 scripts\                 # Move to folder
Move-Item *.log C:\logs\anihpj\               # Move all logs

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DELETE (⚠️ CAREFUL - No Recycle Bin by default!)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Remove-Item tempfile.txt                      # Delete file
Remove-Item -Path important.txt -Confirm      # Ask first
Remove-Item -Path *.pyc                       # Delete pattern

# Delete directory (like rm -r)
Remove-Item -Path old_project -Recurse
Remove-Item -Path __pycache__ -Recurse -Force  # Like rm -rf

# SAFER: Move to Recycle Bin using Shell.Application
$shell = New-Object -ComObject Shell.Application
$shell.Namespace(0).ParseName("C:\path\file.txt").InvokeVerb("delete")
2.3 File Viewing and Manipulation Read, inspect, and navigate file contents
▶️ Viewing Files in DevOps Context
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# cat - Concatenate and display file contents
# Best for: Small files, combining files
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Display entire file
cat requirements.txt

# Display with line numbers
cat -n models.py

# Combine multiple files
cat header.html content.html footer.html > page.html

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# head / tail - View beginning or end of files
# Best for: Log files, large files
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# View first/last 10 lines (default)
head /var/log/nginx/access.log
tail /var/log/nginx/error.log

# Specify number of lines
head -20 settings.py         # First 20 lines
tail -50 debug.log           # Last 50 lines
head -c 1000 large_file.bin  # First 1000 bytes

# ⭐ FOLLOW LOG FILES IN REAL-TIME (Essential for DevOps!)
tail -f /var/log/django/app.log              # Follow one log
tail -f /var/log/nginx/*.log                 # Follow multiple logs
tail -f app.log | grep --line-buffered ERROR # Follow and filter

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# less - Interactive file viewer (pager)
# Best for: Large files, navigation needed
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

less /var/log/syslog         # Open in pager

# Navigation in less:
#   Space / Page Down  - Next page
#   b / Page Up        - Previous page
#   g                  - Go to beginning
#   G                  - Go to end
#   /pattern           - Search forward
#   ?pattern           - Search backward
#   n                  - Next search result
#   N                  - Previous search result
#   q                  - Quit

# View with line numbers
less -N models.py

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PRACTICAL DEVOPS EXAMPLES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Monitor Django logs in real-time while testing
tail -f /var/log/django/anihpj.log | grep -E "(ERROR|WARNING)"

# Check last 100 lines of nginx access log
tail -100 /var/log/nginx/access.log | less

# View deployment configuration
cat deploy/production.env

# Compare first 5 lines of two config files
head -5 settings/base.py settings/production.py

# Watch Docker container logs
docker logs -f anihpj-web --tail 50
📋 File Viewing Decision Tree
                    What do you need?
                           │
           ┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
           │               │               │
      Quick look      Navigate/       Real-time
      at file         Search          monitoring
           │               │               │
           ▼               ▼               ▼
    ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐
    │   cat    │    │   less   │    │ tail -f  │
    │  (small  │    │  (large  │    │  (logs,  │
    │  files)  │    │  files)  │    │ streams) │
    └──────────┘    └──────────┘    └──────────┘
           │
    ┌──────┴──────┐
    │             │
 Beginning      End
    │             │
    ▼             ▼
┌────────┐   ┌────────┐
│  head  │   │  tail  │
│ -n 20  │   │ -n 50  │
└────────┘   └────────┘
🪟 PowerShell Equivalent: File Viewing
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# VIEWING FILES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Display entire file (like cat)
Get-Content requirements.txt
cat requirements.txt                          # Alias works
type requirements.txt                         # Windows traditional

# Display with line numbers (like cat -n)
Get-Content models.py | ForEach-Object { "{0}: {1}" -f $_.ReadCount, $_ }
# Or use Select-String for simplicity:
Get-Content models.py | Select-String "."

# First N lines (like head)
Get-Content app.log -Head 10
Get-Content app.log -TotalCount 10            # Same thing

# Last N lines (like tail)
Get-Content app.log -Tail 20

# Follow file in real-time (like tail -f)
Get-Content app.log -Wait -Tail 10

# Filter while following (like tail -f | grep)
Get-Content app.log -Wait -Tail 10 | Where-Object { $_ -match "ERROR|WARNING" }

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# COMBINING FILES (like cat file1 file2 > output)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Get-Content header.html, content.html, footer.html | Set-Content page.html

# Or with explicit paths:
Get-Content -Path @("header.html", "content.html") | Out-File combined.html
2.4 Text Processing (grep, sed, awk, cut, sort, uniq) Search, filter, and transform text data
🔧 Text Processing Pipeline 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              THE UNIX TEXT PROCESSING PHILOSOPHY                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   Input    ─────▶  Filter  ─────▶  Transform  ─────▶  Output    │
│   (file)          (grep)           (awk/sed)         (file)     │
│                                                                  │
│   ┌────────┐     ┌────────┐      ┌─────────┐      ┌─────────┐  │
│   │ Log    │────▶│ grep   │─────▶│ awk     │─────▶│ Sorted  │  │
│   │ File   │     │ ERROR  │      │ '{$1}'  │      │ Report  │  │
│   └────────┘     └────────┘      └─────────┘      └─────────┘  │
│                                                                  │
│   COMMON PIPELINE:                                               │
│   cat file | grep pattern | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head   │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ grep - Pattern Searching (Most Used!)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# grep - Global Regular Expression Print
# USE: Find lines matching a pattern
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Basic search
grep "ERROR" /var/log/app.log          # Find lines with ERROR
grep -i "error" app.log                # Case-insensitive (-i)
grep -n "def " views.py                # Show line numbers (-n)
grep -c "WARNING" app.log              # Count matches (-c)

# Recursive search (search all files in directory)
grep -r "DATABASE_URL" .               # Search in all files
grep -r "import boto3" --include="*.py" .  # Only .py files
grep -rn "TODO" --include="*.py" ~/projects/anihpj/

# Inverted match (lines NOT matching)
grep -v "DEBUG" app.log                # Exclude DEBUG lines
grep -v "^#" config.txt                # Exclude comments

# Context (show lines before/after match)
grep -B 3 "Exception" app.log          # 3 lines Before
grep -A 5 "ERROR" app.log              # 5 lines After
grep -C 2 "traceback" app.log          # 2 lines Context (both)

# Regular expressions (-E for extended regex)
grep -E "ERROR|WARNING|CRITICAL" log   # OR pattern
grep -E "^2024-01-15" access.log       # Lines starting with date
grep -E "user_[0-9]+" database.log     # Pattern with numbers

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DEVOPS EXAMPLES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Find all 500 errors in nginx logs
grep " 500 " /var/log/nginx/access.log

# Find Django errors with context
grep -B5 -A10 "Traceback" /var/log/django/error.log

# Count requests per status code
grep -oE " [0-9]{3} " access.log | sort | uniq -c

# Find all TODO comments in project
grep -rn "TODO\|FIXME\|XXX" --include="*.py" .
▶️ sed, awk, cut, sort, uniq
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# sed - Stream Editor (Search and Replace)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Basic substitution (first occurrence per line)
sed 's/old/new/' file.txt              # Replace first match
sed 's/old/new/g' file.txt             # Replace ALL matches (global)
sed -i 's/old/new/g' file.txt          # Edit file in-place (-i)

# DevOps examples
sed 's/localhost/production.db.com/g' settings.py
sed -i 's/DEBUG=True/DEBUG=False/g' .env

# Delete lines
sed '/^#/d' config.txt                 # Delete comment lines
sed '/^$/d' file.txt                   # Delete empty lines

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# awk - Pattern Scanning and Processing
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Print specific columns ($1=first, $2=second, $NF=last)
awk '{print $1}' access.log            # First column (IP addresses)
awk '{print $1, $7}' access.log        # IP and URL
awk '{print $NF}' file.txt             # Last column

# With delimiter
awk -F: '{print $1}' /etc/passwd       # Split by colon
awk -F, '{print $2}' users.csv         # Split by comma (CSV)

# Conditional printing
awk '$9 == 500 {print $0}' access.log  # Only 500 status codes
awk '$3 > 1000 {print $1, $3}' data    # Where 3rd field > 1000

# Calculate statistics
awk '{sum+=$1} END {print sum}' numbers.txt  # Sum first column
awk '{sum+=$10} END {print sum/NR}' access.log  # Average

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# cut - Extract columns/fields
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cut -d: -f1 /etc/passwd                # First field, colon delimiter
cut -d, -f2,4 users.csv                # Fields 2 and 4 from CSV
cut -c1-10 file.txt                    # First 10 characters

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# sort & uniq - Sort and count unique values
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Sorting
sort names.txt                         # Alphabetical
sort -n numbers.txt                    # Numeric
sort -r file.txt                       # Reverse
sort -t: -k3 -n /etc/passwd            # Sort by 3rd field, numeric

# Unique values (MUST sort first!)
sort file.txt | uniq                   # Remove duplicates
sort file.txt | uniq -c                # Count occurrences
sort file.txt | uniq -d                # Show only duplicates

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# COMBINED PIPELINE EXAMPLE
# Find top 10 IP addresses hitting your server
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

awk '{print $1}' /var/log/nginx/access.log | \
    sort | \
    uniq -c | \
    sort -rn | \
    head -10

# Output:
#   4523 192.168.1.100
#   3201 10.0.0.45
#   1876 172.16.0.12
#   ...
🪟 PowerShell Equivalent: Text Processing
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SELECT-STRING - Like grep (Pattern Searching)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Basic search
Select-String -Pattern "ERROR" -Path app.log
Select-String "ERROR" app.log                  # Short form

# Case-insensitive (default!) - use -CaseSensitive if needed
Select-String "error" app.log                  # Already case-insensitive

# Show line numbers (default!)
Select-String "def " views.py                  # Shows line numbers

# Recursive search (like grep -r)
Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter *.py | Select-String "import os"

# Invert match (like grep -v)
Select-String "DEBUG" app.log -NotMatch

# Context lines (like grep -A/-B/-C)
Select-String "ERROR" app.log -Context 2,2     # 2 before, 2 after

# Multiple patterns (like grep -E)
Select-String -Pattern "ERROR|WARNING|CRITICAL" app.log

# Count matches (like grep -c)
(Select-String "ERROR" app.log).Count

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# -REPLACE - Like sed (Text Substitution)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Simple replacement
(Get-Content settings.py) -replace 'DEBUG=True', 'DEBUG=False'

# Replace and save
(Get-Content settings.py) -replace 'old', 'new' | Set-Content settings.py

# Regex replacement
"192.168.1.100" -replace '\d+$', 'XXX'         # 192.168.1.XXX

# Multiple replacements
$content = Get-Content config.txt
$content = $content -replace 'localhost', 'production.server.com'
$content = $content -replace 'debug', 'info'
$content | Set-Content config.txt

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FOREACH-OBJECT - Like awk (Field Processing)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Process each line
Get-Content data.txt | ForEach-Object { $_.Split()[0] }

# Extract specific columns from CSV
Import-Csv users.csv | Select-Object Name, Email

# Parse access logs (space-delimited)
Get-Content access.log | ForEach-Object {
    $fields = $_ -split '\s+'
    [PSCustomObject]@{
        IP = $fields[0]
        Date = $fields[3]
        Status = $fields[8]
    }
}

# Sum values
(Get-Content numbers.txt | Measure-Object -Sum).Sum

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SORTING AND UNIQUE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Sort
Get-Content names.txt | Sort-Object
Get-Content numbers.txt | Sort-Object { [int]$_ }  # Numeric sort
Get-Content file.txt | Sort-Object -Descending     # Reverse

# Unique values
Get-Content file.txt | Sort-Object -Unique         # Remove duplicates

# Count occurrences (like sort | uniq -c)
Get-Content file.txt | Group-Object | Sort-Object Count -Descending

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TOP IPs EXAMPLE (Combined Pipeline)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Get-Content access.log | 
    ForEach-Object { ($_ -split '\s+')[0] } |
    Group-Object |
    Sort-Object Count -Descending |
    Select-Object -First 10 |
    Format-Table Count, Name
2.5 Pipes and Redirection Connect commands and manage input/output streams
🔀 Stream Redirection Diagram 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    STANDARD STREAMS                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│     ┌──────────┐                      ┌──────────────┐          │
│     │ STDIN    │─────────────────────▶│   Command    │          │
│     │ (0)      │     Standard         │              │          │
│     │ Keyboard │     Input            │   Example:   │          │
│     └──────────┘                      │   grep       │          │
│                                       │   sort       │          │
│                                       │   python     │          │
│     ┌──────────┐◀─────────────────────│              │          │
│     │ STDOUT   │     Standard         │              │          │
│     │ (1)      │     Output           │              │          │
│     │ Screen   │                      │              │          │
│     └──────────┘                      │              │          │
│                                       │              │          │
│     ┌──────────┐◀─────────────────────│              │          │
│     │ STDERR   │     Standard         └──────────────┘          │
│     │ (2)      │     Error                                      │
│     │ Screen   │                                                │
│     └──────────┘                                                │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

REDIRECTION OPERATORS:
  >   Write stdout to file (overwrite)
  >>  Append stdout to file
  <   Read stdin from file
  2>  Write stderr to file
  &>  Write both stdout and stderr to file
  2>&1  Redirect stderr to stdout
▶️ Output Redirection
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# OUTPUT REDIRECTION
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Overwrite file with output
ls -la > directory_listing.txt
python manage.py check > check_results.txt

# Append to file (don't overwrite)
echo "Deploy started at $(date)" >> deploy.log
echo "Step 1 complete" >> deploy.log

# Redirect errors separately
python manage.py migrate 2> migration_errors.txt

# Redirect both stdout and stderr
python manage.py test &> test_output.txt
# OR (older syntax)
python manage.py test > test_output.txt 2>&1

# Redirect stdout and stderr to different files
python manage.py runserver \
    > server.log \
    2> server_errors.log

# Discard output (send to "black hole")
command > /dev/null              # Discard stdout
command 2> /dev/null             # Discard stderr
command &> /dev/null             # Discard everything

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# INPUT REDIRECTION
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Read from file instead of keyboard
python script.py < input_data.txt
mysql -u root < database_backup.sql

# Here Document (multi-line input)
cat << EOF > config.txt
DATABASE_URL=postgres://localhost/anihpj
DEBUG=False
ALLOWED_HOSTS=*.example.com
EOF

# Here String (single-line input)
grep -c "error" <<< "This is an error message"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PIPES - Connect commands
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Basic pipe: output of command1 becomes input of command2
ls -la | grep ".py"              # List then filter
cat log.txt | sort | uniq        # Read, sort, unique

# Multiple pipes (pipeline)
cat access.log | \
    grep "POST /api" | \
    awk '{print $1}' | \
    sort | \
    uniq -c | \
    sort -rn | \
    head -5

# Practical DevOps pipelines
# Find largest files
du -ah /var/log | sort -rh | head -10

# Count errors by type
grep ERROR app.log | cut -d: -f2 | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

# Check who's logged in to servers
cat servers.txt | xargs -I {} ssh {} "hostname; who"
📋 Redirection Cheat Sheet
┌──────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Operator         │ Description                                  │
├──────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ cmd > file       │ Redirect stdout to file (overwrite)         │
│ cmd >> file      │ Redirect stdout to file (append)            │
│ cmd < file       │ Redirect stdin from file                    │
│ cmd 2> file      │ Redirect stderr to file                     │
│ cmd 2>> file     │ Redirect stderr to file (append)            │
│ cmd &> file      │ Redirect both stdout & stderr to file       │
│ cmd 2>&1         │ Redirect stderr to stdout                   │
│ cmd > /dev/null  │ Discard stdout                              │
│ cmd1 | cmd2      │ Pipe: stdout of cmd1 → stdin of cmd2        │
│ cmd1 | tee file  │ Send to both stdout AND file                │
└──────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TEE - Write to both file AND screen:
  command | tee output.txt           # Write and display
  command | tee -a output.txt        # Append and display
  command 2>&1 | tee debug.log       # Capture everything
🪟 PowerShell Equivalent: Pipes & Redirection
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PIPE - Same concept, but passes OBJECTS not text
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Pipe works the same way
Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.CPU -gt 100 } | Sort-Object CPU -Descending

# Multiple pipes
Get-ChildItem -Recurse | Where-Object { $_.Extension -eq ".log" } |
    Sort-Object Length -Descending | Select-Object -First 10

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# REDIRECTION
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Write to file (overwrite) - like >
Get-Process | Out-File processes.txt
"Hello" > output.txt                           # Short form

# Append to file - like >>
"More content" >> output.txt
"More" | Out-File output.txt -Append

# Redirect errors
Get-Process -Name "nonexistent" 2> errors.txt  # Errors to file
Get-Process -Name "nonexistent" 2>&1          # Errors to output

# Redirect all streams (like &>)
Get-Process *>&1 | Out-File all.txt

# Discard output (like > /dev/null)
Get-Process | Out-Null
$null = Get-Process                            # Faster method

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEE-OBJECT - Write to screen AND file
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Get-Process | Tee-Object -FilePath processes.txt
Get-Content log.txt | Tee-Object -Variable saved | Select-String "ERROR"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# POWERSHELL STREAM NUMBERS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 1 - Success (stdout)    4 - Verbose
# 2 - Error   (stderr)    5 - Debug
# 3 - Warning             6 - Information

# Redirect specific streams
Get-Process 3> warnings.txt                    # Warnings only
Get-Process *>&1                               # All streams to success
2.6 Process Management Monitor and control running processes
🔄 Process Lifecycle 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    PROCESS STATES                                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   ┌─────────┐    fork()    ┌─────────┐    schedule   ┌───────┐ │
│   │ Parent  │─────────────▶│  Ready  │──────────────▶│Running│ │
│   │ Process │              │ (New)   │               │       │ │
│   └─────────┘              └─────────┘               └───┬───┘ │
│                                  ▲                       │      │
│                                  │                       │      │
│                           wake   │        I/O wait       │      │
│                                  │         sleep         ▼      │
│                            ┌─────┴─────┐          ┌─────────┐  │
│                            │  Waiting  │◀─────────│ Blocked │  │
│                            │  (Sleep)  │          │  (I/O)  │  │
│                            └───────────┘          └─────────┘  │
│                                                        │        │
│                                        exit()  ┌──────▼──────┐ │
│                                       ─────────│   Zombie    │ │
│                                                │ (Terminated)│ │
│                                                └─────────────┘ │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Process Monitoring Commands
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ps - Process Status (snapshot)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Show your processes
ps                              # Basic (current terminal only)
ps aux                          # All processes, detailed
ps -ef                          # Full format listing

# Find specific processes
ps aux | grep python            # Find Python processes
ps aux | grep gunicorn          # Find Gunicorn workers
ps -ef | grep -v grep | grep nginx  # Exclude grep itself

# Useful ps columns
# USER  PID  %CPU  %MEM  VSZ  RSS  TTY  STAT  START  TIME  COMMAND

# Show process tree (parent-child relationships)
ps auxf                         # Tree format with aux
pstree                          # Visual tree
pstree -p                       # Include PIDs

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# top / htop - Real-time process monitoring
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

top                             # Basic real-time view
htop                            # Better interface (install first)

# Top commands (while running):
#   M     - Sort by memory
#   P     - Sort by CPU
#   k     - Kill a process (enter PID)
#   q     - Quit
#   1     - Show individual CPUs
#   c     - Show full command

# One-shot top (for scripts)
top -bn1 | head -20             # Batch mode, 1 iteration

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DJANGO/GUNICORN PROCESS CHECK
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Check if Django is running
ps aux | grep "manage.py runserver"

# Check Gunicorn workers
ps aux | grep gunicorn
# Output might show:
# gunicorn: master [anihpj.wsgi]
# gunicorn: worker [anihpj.wsgi]
# gunicorn: worker [anihpj.wsgi]

# Count Gunicorn workers
ps aux | grep "gunicorn: worker" | grep -v grep | wc -l
▶️ Process Control (kill, signals)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# kill - Send signals to processes
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Common signals
kill PID                        # Default: SIGTERM (15) - graceful
kill -9 PID                     # SIGKILL (9) - force kill
kill -HUP PID                   # SIGHUP (1) - reload config

# Kill by name
pkill python                    # Kill all Python processes
pkill -f "manage.py runserver"  # Match full command
killall gunicorn                # Kill all gunicorn processes

# ⚠️ Signal reference
# SIGTERM (15) - Please terminate (graceful shutdown)
# SIGKILL (9)  - Force kill (cannot be caught/ignored)
# SIGHUP (1)   - Hangup (often used to reload config)
# SIGINT (2)   - Interrupt (like Ctrl+C)
# SIGSTOP (19) - Pause process
# SIGCONT (18) - Continue paused process

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Background Processes
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Run in background
python long_script.py &          # Ampersand runs in background
./deploy.sh &                    # Deploy script in background

# Keep running after logout (nohup)
nohup python long_task.py &      # Continues after you logout
nohup ./backup.sh > backup.log 2>&1 &  # With logging

# Job control
jobs                            # List background jobs
fg %1                           # Bring job 1 to foreground
bg %1                           # Send stopped job to background
Ctrl+Z                          # Suspend current process

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DEVOPS EXAMPLES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Gracefully reload Nginx
sudo kill -HUP $(cat /var/run/nginx.pid)
# OR
sudo nginx -s reload

# Gracefully reload Gunicorn
kill -HUP $(cat /var/run/gunicorn.pid)

# Force kill stuck process
kill -9 $(pgrep -f "stuck_script.py")

# Monitor Django while deploying
top -p $(pgrep -f gunicorn | head -1)
🪟 PowerShell Equivalent: Process Management
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# VIEWING PROCESSES (like ps)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Get-Process                                    # List all processes
Get-Process python                             # By name
Get-Process -Id 1234                           # By PID

# Detailed info (like ps aux)
Get-Process | Select-Object Id, ProcessName, CPU, WorkingSet | Format-Table

# Filter high CPU (like ps aux | sort CPU | tail)
Get-Process | Sort-Object CPU -Descending | Select-Object -First 10

# Find process by command line (like ps aux | grep)
Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.Path -like "*python*" }
Get-CimInstance Win32_Process | Where-Object { $_.CommandLine -like "*gunicorn*" }

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# KILLING PROCESSES (like kill)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Stop-Process -Id 1234                          # By PID
Stop-Process -Name python                      # By name (kills ALL)
Stop-Process -Name python -Force               # Force kill (-9)

# Kill by pattern (like pkill -f)
Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.ProcessName -like "*python*" } | Stop-Process

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BACKGROUND JOBS (like & and nohup)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Start background job
Start-Job -ScriptBlock { python long_script.py }

# Check job status
Get-Job                                        # List all jobs
Get-Job -Id 1 | Receive-Job                    # Get output

# Run and detach (like nohup)
Start-Process python -ArgumentList "long_task.py" -NoNewWindow -RedirectStandardOutput "output.log"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# REAL-TIME MONITORING (like top)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Simple top-like view (refreshes once)
Get-Process | Sort-Object CPU -Descending | Select-Object -First 20

# Continuous monitoring (watch style)
while ($true) { 
    Clear-Host
    Get-Process | Sort-Object CPU -Descending | Select-Object -First 20 | Format-Table
    Start-Sleep 2
}
2.7 Environment Variables and PATH Configure your shell environment
▶️ Environment Variables
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# VIEWING ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Show all environment variables
env                             # All variables
printenv                        # Same as env
set                             # All variables including local

# Show specific variable
echo $HOME                      # /home/developer
echo $USER                      # developer
echo $PATH                      # Command search path
echo $SHELL                     # Current shell
echo $PWD                       # Current directory

printenv HOME                   # Alternative syntax

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SETTING ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Temporary (current session only)
export DATABASE_URL="postgres://localhost/anihpj"
export DEBUG=False
export DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE="anihpj.settings.production"

# For single command only
DEBUG=True python manage.py runserver
DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///test.db python manage.py test

# Permanent (add to ~/.bashrc or ~/.bash_profile)
echo 'export EDITOR=vim' >> ~/.bashrc
source ~/.bashrc                # Reload config

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# THE PATH VARIABLE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# PATH = Where shell looks for commands
echo $PATH
# /usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/home/developer/.local/bin

# Add directory to PATH
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"          # Prepend (higher priority)
export PATH="$PATH:/opt/custom/bin"           # Append (lower priority)

# Check where command is found
which python                    # /usr/bin/python
which pip                       # /home/developer/.local/bin/pip
type -a python                  # Show ALL matches in PATH

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DJANGO ENVIRONMENT SETUP
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Common Django environment variables
export DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE="anihpj.settings.production"
export SECRET_KEY="your-secret-key-here"
export DATABASE_URL="postgres://user:pass@localhost:5432/anihpj"
export REDIS_URL="redis://localhost:6379/0"
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID="AKIA..."
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY="..."
export DEBUG=False
export ALLOWED_HOSTS="example.com,www.example.com"

# Load from .env file (using export)
export $(grep -v '^#' .env | xargs)
📋 Important Environment Variables
┌─────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Variable            │ Purpose                                    │
├─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ HOME                │ User's home directory                      │
│ USER                │ Current username                           │
│ PATH                │ Directories to search for commands         │
│ SHELL               │ Current shell program                      │
│ PWD                 │ Present working directory                  │
│ EDITOR              │ Default text editor                        │
│ LANG                │ System language/locale                     │
├─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ DJANGO SPECIFIC:    │                                            │
├─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE │ Which settings file to use             │
│ SECRET_KEY          │ Django secret key                          │
│ DATABASE_URL        │ Database connection string                 │
│ DEBUG               │ Enable/disable debug mode                  │
│ ALLOWED_HOSTS       │ Valid hostnames for requests               │
├─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ AWS SPECIFIC:       │                                            │
├─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID   │ AWS credentials                            │
│ AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY │ AWS credentials                          │
│ AWS_DEFAULT_REGION  │ Default AWS region                         │
│ AWS_PROFILE         │ Named credentials profile                  │
└─────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────┘
🪟 PowerShell Equivalent: Environment Variables
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# VIEWING ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Show all environment variables
Get-ChildItem Env:                             # List all
dir env:                                       # Alias
$env:PATH                                      # Show specific variable

# Common variables (different names in Windows!)
$env:USERPROFILE                               # Like $HOME
$env:USERNAME                                  # Like $USER
$env:PATH                                      # Same concept
$env:COMPUTERNAME                              # Hostname

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SETTING ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Temporary (current session only)
$env:DATABASE_URL = "postgres://localhost/anihpj"
$env:DEBUG = "False"
$env:DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "anihpj.settings.production"

# Check if set
if ($env:DEBUG -eq "True") { Write-Host "Debug mode ON" }

# Set for a single command (different approach)
$env:DEBUG = "True"; python manage.py runserver; $env:DEBUG = $null

# Permanent - User level (persists after restart)
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("EDITOR", "code", "User")

# Permanent - Machine level (requires admin)
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("MY_APP_KEY", "value", "Machine")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# THE PATH VARIABLE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# View PATH (semicolon-separated on Windows)
$env:PATH -split ';'

# Add to PATH (session only)
$env:PATH = "C:\tools\bin;$env:PATH"           # Prepend
$env:PATH = "$env:PATH;C:\tools\bin"           # Append

# Add to PATH permanently
$currentPath = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("PATH", "User")
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("PATH", "$currentPath;C:\tools\bin", "User")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DJANGO SETUP IN POWERSHELL
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

$env:DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "anihpj.settings.production"
$env:SECRET_KEY = "your-secret-key-here"
$env:DATABASE_URL = "postgres://user:pass@localhost:5432/anihpj"

# Load from .env file
Get-Content .env | ForEach-Object {
    if ($_ -match '^\s*([^#][^=]+)=(.*)$') {
        [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable($matches[1].Trim(), $matches[2].Trim())
    }
}
2.8 Command History and Aliases Work faster with history and shortcuts
▶️ Command History
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# COMMAND HISTORY
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# View history
history                         # Show all history
history 20                      # Last 20 commands
history | grep deploy           # Find deploy commands

# Navigate history
↑ / ↓                           # Previous/next commands
Ctrl+R                          # Reverse search (VERY USEFUL!)
                                # Start typing, matches appear

# History expansion
!!                              # Repeat last command
sudo !!                         # Re-run last command with sudo
!n                              # Run command #n from history
!-2                             # Run command 2 back
!$                              # Last argument of previous command
!*                              # All arguments of previous command

# Examples
cat settings.py
vim !$                          # vim settings.py (last arg)

ls /var/log/nginx/
cd !$                           # cd /var/log/nginx/

# Clear history
history -c                      # Clear current session
rm ~/.bash_history              # Clear history file

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ALIASES - Command Shortcuts
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Create alias (temporary)
alias ll='ls -la'
alias gs='git status'
alias py='python3'

# Make permanent (add to ~/.bashrc)
echo "alias ll='ls -la'" >> ~/.bashrc
echo "alias deploy='./scripts/deploy.sh'" >> ~/.bashrc
source ~/.bashrc

# List all aliases
alias

# Remove alias
unalias ll

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DEVOPS ALIASES (Add these to ~/.bashrc)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Navigation
alias proj='cd ~/projects/anihpj'
alias logs='cd /var/log'

# Git shortcuts
alias gs='git status'
alias gp='git pull'
alias gc='git commit -m'
alias gd='git diff'

# Django shortcuts
alias pm='python manage.py'
alias pmr='python manage.py runserver'
alias pmt='python manage.py test'
alias pmm='python manage.py migrate'
alias pmmm='python manage.py makemigrations'
alias pmsh='python manage.py shell'

# Docker shortcuts
alias dps='docker ps'
alias dlog='docker logs -f'
alias dex='docker exec -it'

# Kubernetes shortcuts
alias k='kubectl'
alias kgp='kubectl get pods'
alias kgs='kubectl get services'
alias klog='kubectl logs -f'

# Safety aliases (ask before overwrite)
alias cp='cp -i'
alias mv='mv -i'
alias rm='rm -i'
2.9 Remote Command Execution (SSH) Securely connect and execute commands on remote servers
🔐 SSH Key Authentication Flow 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              SSH KEY-BASED AUTHENTICATION                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   YOUR MACHINE                        REMOTE SERVER              │
│   ┌─────────────────┐                ┌─────────────────┐        │
│   │ ~/.ssh/         │                │ ~/.ssh/         │        │
│   │                 │                │                 │        │
│   │  id_rsa         │────────────────│  authorized_keys│        │
│   │  (PRIVATE KEY)  │  Must match    │  (PUBLIC KEY)   │        │
│   │  Keep SECRET!   │                │  Can share      │        │
│   │                 │                │                 │        │
│   │  id_rsa.pub     │─── Copy to ───▶│  authorized_keys│        │
│   │  (PUBLIC KEY)   │                │                 │        │
│   └─────────────────┘                └─────────────────┘        │
│                                                                  │
│   CONNECTION FLOW:                                               │
│   1. Client sends public key ID                                  │
│   2. Server checks authorized_keys                               │
│   3. Server sends encrypted challenge                            │
│   4. Client decrypts with private key                            │
│   5. Server verifies → Connection established                    │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ SSH Basics
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SSH KEY SETUP (One-time)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Generate SSH key pair
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "developer@example.com"
# OR (older RSA format)
ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "developer@example.com"

# This creates:
# ~/.ssh/id_ed25519      (private key - NEVER share)
# ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub  (public key - share with servers)

# Copy public key to server
ssh-copy-id user@server.example.com
# OR manually:
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub | ssh user@server "mkdir -p ~/.ssh && cat >> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SSH CONNECTIONS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Basic connection
ssh user@hostname
ssh developer@production.anihpj.com
ssh -p 2222 user@server              # Custom port

# Execute single command (doesn't open shell)
ssh user@server "ls -la"
ssh user@server "hostname && uptime"
ssh user@server "docker ps"

# Execute command and exit
ssh user@server "cd /app && git pull && sudo systemctl restart gunicorn"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SSH CONFIG FILE (~/.ssh/config)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Create config for easy connections
cat << 'EOF' > ~/.ssh/config
# Production server
Host prod
    HostName production.anihpj.com
    User deploy
    Port 22
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

# Staging server
Host staging
    HostName staging.anihpj.com
    User deploy
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

# Jump host (bastion)
Host bastion
    HostName bastion.anihpj.com
    User admin
    
# Internal server via bastion
Host internal
    HostName 10.0.1.100
    User deploy
    ProxyJump bastion
EOF

# Now you can simply type:
ssh prod                            # Instead of full command
ssh staging
ssh internal                        # Auto-jumps through bastion

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FILE TRANSFER (SCP and RSYNC)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# SCP - Secure Copy
scp file.txt user@server:/path/          # Upload
scp user@server:/path/file.txt .         # Download
scp -r folder/ user@server:/path/        # Directory

# Rsync - Better for large/repeated transfers
rsync -avz local/ user@server:/remote/   # Sync directory
rsync -avz --delete src/ dest/           # Mirror (delete extras)
rsync -avzP large_file.tar user@server:  # Progress bar (-P)

# Deploy Django project
rsync -avz --exclude='.git' --exclude='*.pyc' \
    --exclude='__pycache__' --exclude='.env' \
    ./ deploy@prod:/var/www/anihpj/
📋 SSH Quick Reference
┌────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Command                    │ Description                        │
├────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ssh user@host              │ Connect to remote server           │
│ ssh -p 2222 user@host      │ Connect on custom port             │
│ ssh user@host "command"    │ Run command on remote              │
│ ssh-keygen -t ed25519      │ Generate SSH key pair              │
│ ssh-copy-id user@host      │ Copy public key to server          │
│ scp file user@host:/path   │ Copy file to server                │
│ rsync -avz src/ host:dest/ │ Sync directories                   │
│ ssh -L 8080:localhost:80   │ Local port forwarding              │
│ ssh -D 1080 user@host      │ SOCKS proxy (tunnel)               │
└────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────┘

SSH CONFIG FILE KEYWORDS:
  Host         - Alias for the connection
  HostName     - Actual hostname/IP
  User         - Username to login as
  Port         - SSH port (default 22)
  IdentityFile - Path to private key
  ProxyJump    - Jump through another host first

Chapter 3: Version Control for Scripts & Automation

Git best practices for infrastructure and automation code

Chapter Topics

3.1 Git Fundamentals for DevOps
3.2 Branching Strategies for Infrastructure Code
3.3 Git Hooks for Automation
3.4 Repository Structure Best Practices
3.5 Secrets Management in Git
3.6 Code Review Workflows
3.7 Semantic Versioning for Scripts
3.8 Monorepo vs Polyrepo for Automation Code
3.1 Git Fundamentals for DevOps Version control essentials for automation code
🔀 Git Workflow Visual 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     GIT AREAS                                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   WORKING         STAGING          LOCAL           REMOTE        │
│   DIRECTORY       AREA             REPOSITORY      REPOSITORY    │
│   ┌─────────┐    ┌─────────┐      ┌─────────┐    ┌─────────┐   │
│   │         │    │         │      │         │    │         │   │
│   │  Edit   │───▶│  Stage  │─────▶│ Commit  │───▶│  Push   │   │
│   │  Files  │add │ Changes │commit│ History │push│ GitHub  │   │
│   │         │    │         │      │         │    │         │   │
│   └─────────┘    └─────────┘      └─────────┘    └─────────┘   │
│        │              │                │              │         │
│        │◀─────────────┘                │              │         │
│        │    git restore               │              │         │
│        │                               │              │         │
│        │◀──────────────────────────────┘              │         │
│        │         git reset                           │         │
│        │                                              │         │
│        │◀─────────────────────────────────────────────┘         │
│                          git pull                                │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Essential Git Commands for DevOps
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# INITIAL SETUP
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Configure identity (required for commits)
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "your.email@example.com"

# Useful global settings
git config --global init.defaultBranch main
git config --global pull.rebase false
git config --global core.editor "code --wait"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DAILY WORKFLOW
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Clone a repository
git clone https://github.com/org/anihpj.git
git clone git@github.com:org/anihpj.git     # SSH (preferred)

# Check status (use frequently!)
git status                      # Full status
git status -s                   # Short format

# Stage changes
git add file.py                 # Stage specific file
git add .                       # Stage all changes
git add -p                      # Interactive staging (review changes)

# Commit with message
git commit -m "Add health check endpoint"
git commit -am "Fix bug"        # Stage tracked files + commit

# Push to remote
git push origin main
git push -u origin feature-branch  # Set upstream

# Pull latest changes
git pull                        # Fetch + merge
git pull --rebase               # Fetch + rebase (cleaner history)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# VIEWING HISTORY
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Log formats
git log                         # Full log
git log --oneline               # Compact (one line per commit)
git log --oneline -10           # Last 10 commits
git log --graph --oneline       # Visual branch graph
git log -p                      # Show diffs

# Show specific commit
git show abc123                 # Show commit details
git diff                        # Unstaged changes
git diff --staged               # Staged changes
git diff main..feature          # Diff between branches
3.2 Branching Strategies for Infrastructure Code Organize development and deployment branches
🌲 Git Flow for Infrastructure 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              BRANCHING STRATEGY FOR DEVOPS                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   main (production)                                              │
│   ────●────────●─────────────●─────────────●────────────▶       │
│       │        ↑             ↑             ↑                     │
│       │      merge         merge         merge                   │
│       │        │             │             │                     │
│   staging     │             │             │                     │
│   ────●───────●─────────────●─────────────●─────────────▶       │
│       │       ↑             ↑             ↑                     │
│       │     merge         merge         merge                   │
│       │       │             │             │                     │
│   develop    │             │             │                     │
│   ────●──●──●─●──●──●──●──●─●──●──●──●──●─●──●──●───────▶       │
│          ↑  ↑    ↑  ↑  ↑      ↑  ↑                              │
│          │  │    │  │  │      │  │                              │
│   feature│  │    │  │  │      │  │                              │
│   branches  │    │  │  │      │  │                              │
│   ──●──●──●─┘    │  │  │      │  │                              │
│                  │  │  │      │  │                              │
│   ─────●────●────┘  │  │      │  │                              │
│                     │  │      │  │                              │
│   ───●──────●───────┘  │      │  │                              │
│                        │      │  │                              │
│   hotfix              │      │  │                              │
│   ─────────────●──────┴──────┘  │                              │
│                                  │                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

BRANCH PURPOSES:
  main     → Production-ready code (auto-deploys)
  staging  → Pre-production testing
  develop  → Integration branch
  feature/ → New features/changes
  hotfix/  → Emergency production fixes
▶️ Branch Operations
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BRANCH MANAGEMENT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# List branches
git branch                      # Local branches
git branch -r                   # Remote branches
git branch -a                   # All branches

# Create and switch
git checkout -b feature/add-caching      # Create + switch
git switch -c feature/add-caching        # Modern syntax

# Switch branches
git checkout develop
git switch develop              # Modern syntax

# Delete branches
git branch -d feature/completed          # Delete merged branch
git branch -D feature/abandoned          # Force delete
git push origin --delete feature/old     # Delete remote branch

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# MERGING AND REBASING
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Merge feature into develop
git checkout develop
git merge feature/add-caching

# Rebase (for cleaner history)
git checkout feature/add-caching
git rebase develop

# Interactive rebase (squash commits)
git rebase -i HEAD~3            # Last 3 commits
# In editor: change 'pick' to 'squash' for commits to combine

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DEVOPS WORKFLOW EXAMPLE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# 1. Start new feature
git checkout develop
git pull origin develop
git checkout -b feature/terraform-eks-module

# 2. Make changes and commit
# ... edit terraform files ...
git add .
git commit -m "Add EKS cluster module"

# 3. Keep branch updated with develop
git fetch origin
git rebase origin/develop

# 4. Push for code review (PR)
git push -u origin feature/terraform-eks-module

# 5. After approval and merge, cleanup
git checkout develop
git pull
git branch -d feature/terraform-eks-module
3.3 Git Hooks for Automation Automate tasks on git events
▶️ Git Hook Examples
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# GIT HOOKS LOCATION: .git/hooks/
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Common hooks:
# pre-commit    - Before commit (lint, format)
# commit-msg    - Validate commit message
# pre-push      - Before push (run tests)
# post-merge    - After merge (install deps)
# post-checkout - After checkout (setup env)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PRE-COMMIT HOOK: Lint and Format
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat > .git/hooks/pre-commit << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Pre-commit hook: Run linting and formatting

echo "🔍 Running pre-commit checks..."

# Check Python syntax
python -m py_compile $(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM | grep '\.py$')
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "❌ Python syntax errors found"
    exit 1
fi

# Run Black formatter check
black --check $(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM | grep '\.py$') 2>/dev/null
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "❌ Code not formatted. Run: black ."
    exit 1
fi

# Check for secrets
if git diff --cached | grep -E "(password|secret|api_key)\s*=\s*['\"]" ; then
    echo "❌ Potential secrets detected in commit!"
    exit 1
fi

echo "✅ Pre-commit checks passed"
exit 0
EOF

chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PRE-PUSH HOOK: Run Tests
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat > .git/hooks/pre-push << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Pre-push hook: Run tests before pushing

echo "🧪 Running tests before push..."

python manage.py test --failfast
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "❌ Tests failed! Push aborted."
    exit 1
fi

echo "✅ Tests passed, pushing..."
exit 0
EOF

chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-push

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# USING PRE-COMMIT FRAMEWORK (Recommended)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Install pre-commit
pip install pre-commit

# Create .pre-commit-config.yaml
cat > .pre-commit-config.yaml << 'EOF'
repos:
  - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks
    rev: v4.5.0
    hooks:
      - id: trailing-whitespace
      - id: end-of-file-fixer
      - id: check-yaml
      - id: check-json
      - id: check-added-large-files
      - id: detect-private-key

  - repo: https://github.com/psf/black
    rev: 24.1.0
    hooks:
      - id: black

  - repo: https://github.com/pycqa/flake8
    rev: 7.0.0
    hooks:
      - id: flake8

  - repo: https://github.com/antonbabenko/pre-commit-terraform
    rev: v1.86.0
    hooks:
      - id: terraform_fmt
      - id: terraform_validate
EOF

# Install hooks
pre-commit install

# Run on all files
pre-commit run --all-files
3.4 Repository Structure Best Practices Organize automation and infrastructure code
📁 DevOps Repository Structure 
anihpj-infrastructure/
├── .github/
│   ├── workflows/              # GitHub Actions CI/CD
│   │   ├── terraform.yml       # Infrastructure CI
│   │   ├── deploy.yml          # Application deployment
│   │   └── security-scan.yml   # Security scanning
│   ├── CODEOWNERS              # Required reviewers
│   └── pull_request_template.md
│
├── terraform/                  # Infrastructure as Code
│   ├── environments/
│   │   ├── dev/
│   │   │   ├── main.tf
│   │   │   └── terraform.tfvars
│   │   ├── staging/
│   │   └── production/
│   ├── modules/                # Reusable modules
│   │   ├── eks/
│   │   ├── rds/
│   │   └── vpc/
│   └── README.md
│
├── ansible/                    # Configuration Management
│   ├── inventory/
│   │   ├── dev.yml
│   │   ├── staging.yml
│   │   └── production.yml
│   ├── playbooks/
│   │   ├── deploy.yml
│   │   └── configure.yml
│   └── roles/
│       ├── nginx/
│       └── app/
│
├── kubernetes/                 # K8s Manifests
│   ├── base/                   # Base configs (Kustomize)
│   │   ├── deployment.yaml
│   │   ├── service.yaml
│   │   └── kustomization.yaml
│   └── overlays/               # Environment overrides
│       ├── dev/
│       ├── staging/
│       └── production/
│
├── scripts/                    # Automation Scripts
│   ├── deploy.sh
│   ├── backup.sh
│   ├── healthcheck.py
│   └── utils/
│       ├── aws_helpers.py
│       └── log_parser.py
│
├── docker/                     # Docker Configs
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   ├── Dockerfile.dev
│   └── docker-compose.yml
│
├── docs/                       # Documentation
│   ├── architecture.md
│   ├── runbooks/
│   │   ├── deployment.md
│   │   └── incident-response.md
│   └── adr/                    # Architecture Decision Records
│       └── 001-use-eks.md
│
├── .gitignore
├── .pre-commit-config.yaml
├── Makefile                    # Common commands
└── README.md
3.5 Secrets Management in Git Keep secrets out of version control
⚠️ NEVER commit secrets to Git!
  • API keys, passwords, tokens
  • .env files with real credentials
  • Private SSH keys
  • Database connection strings with passwords
▶️ .gitignore for DevOps Projects
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# COMPREHENSIVE .GITIGNORE FOR DJANGO + DEVOPS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat > .gitignore << 'EOF'
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SECRETS - CRITICAL: Never commit these!
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
.env
.env.*
!.env.example           # Template IS ok to commit
*.pem
*.key
secrets/
**/secrets/
credentials.json
service-account.json

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# PYTHON / DJANGO
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
__pycache__/
*.py[cod]
*$py.class
*.so
venv/
.venv/
env/
.python-version
*.egg-info/
dist/
build/
.coverage
htmlcov/
.pytest_cache/
.mypy_cache/
db.sqlite3

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# TERRAFORM
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
*.tfstate
*.tfstate.*
*.tfvars
!*.tfvars.example
.terraform/
.terraform.lock.hcl
crash.log
override.tf
override.tf.json

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ANSIBLE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
*.retry
inventory/*
!inventory/*.example

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# IDE / EDITOR
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
.idea/
.vscode/
*.swp
*.swo
*~

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# LOGS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
*.log
logs/
EOF

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CREATE .env.example TEMPLATE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat > .env.example << 'EOF'
# Copy this file to .env and fill in real values
# NEVER commit .env with real secrets!

# Django
DEBUG=False
SECRET_KEY=your-secret-key-here
ALLOWED_HOSTS=localhost,127.0.0.1

# Database
DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:password@localhost:5432/anihpj

# AWS
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-access-key
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-secret-key
AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=us-east-1

# Redis
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379/0
EOF

# Commit the template
git add .env.example
git commit -m "Add environment template"

🐚 Part 2: Bash/Shell Scripting

Master Bash scripting from basics to advanced techniques. Covers variables, control flow, loops, functions, arrays, and professional bash patterns.

🪟 Windows Users

This section teaches Bash (Linux shell scripting). Two options:

  • Use WSL (Recommended): Follow the Windows Setup Guide to install WSL and run these examples natively in Ubuntu
  • Use PowerShell: Skip to Part 4: PowerShell for Windows-native scripting with equivalent functionality

Tip: Learning both Bash and PowerShell makes you more versatile. Bash for Linux servers/Docker/CI-CD, PowerShell for Windows administration.

Chapter 4: Bash Scripting Basics

Foundation skills for writing effective bash scripts

Chapter Topics

4.1 Writing Your First Bash Script
4.2 Variables and Data Types
4.3 Quoting: Single, Double, and Backticks
4.4 Command Substitution $() and ``
4.5 Arithmetic Operations
4.6 Reading User Input
4.7 Exit Codes and Script Status
4.8 Comments and Documentation
4.9 Script Templates and Boilerplate
4.1 Writing Your First Bash Script Create and run your first executable script
📝 Anatomy of a Bash Script 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  BASH SCRIPT STRUCTURE                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   #!/bin/bash                    ← SHEBANG (interpreter)        │
│   # Script description           ← COMMENTS                     │
│   # Author: Your Name                                           │
│                                                                  │
│   set -euo pipefail              ← STRICT MODE (safety)         │
│                                                                  │
│   # Global variables             ← CONFIGURATION                │
│   ENVIRONMENT="production"                                       │
│   LOG_FILE="/var/log/script.log"                                │
│                                                                  │
│   # Functions                    ← REUSABLE CODE                │
│   function log() {                                              │
│       echo "[$(date)] $1"                                       │
│   }                                                             │
│                                                                  │
│   # Main logic                   ← SCRIPT BODY                  │
│   log "Starting..."                                             │
│   # ... your commands ...                                       │
│   log "Done!"                                                   │
│                                                                  │
│   exit 0                         ← EXIT CODE                    │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Create Your First Script
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 1: Create the script file
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat > hello.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# My first bash script
# Description: Prints a greeting

echo "Hello, World!"
echo "Today is $(date)"
echo "You are logged in as: $USER"
EOF

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 2: Make it executable
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

chmod +x hello.sh
# OR
chmod 755 hello.sh

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 3: Run the script
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

./hello.sh                    # Execute from current directory
bash hello.sh                 # Execute with bash explicitly
/full/path/to/hello.sh        # Execute with full path

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SHEBANG VARIATIONS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

#!/bin/bash        # Most common - uses /bin/bash
#!/usr/bin/bash    # Some systems have bash here
#!/bin/sh          # POSIX shell (more portable, less features)
#!/usr/bin/env bash  # Best for portability (finds bash in PATH)
4.2 Variables and Data Types Store and use data in your scripts
▶️ Variable Basics
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DECLARING VARIABLES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Basic assignment (NO spaces around =)
name="John"                    # ✅ Correct
count=42
is_active=true

# name = "John"                # ❌ WRONG - spaces cause error!

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# USING VARIABLES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

echo $name                     # Simple usage
echo "$name"                   # Quoted (safer, preserves whitespace)
echo "${name}"                 # Braced (required for clarity)
echo "Hello, ${name}!"         # In strings

# Braces required when:
file="data"
echo "${file}txt"              # datatxt (without braces: looks for $filetxt)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# VARIABLE TYPES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Strings (default type)
greeting="Hello World"
path="/var/log/app.log"

# Numbers (treated as strings, but can do arithmetic)
count=10
port=8000

# Arrays
servers=("web1" "web2" "web3")
echo "${servers[0]}"           # First element

# Read-only (constant)
readonly PI=3.14159
readonly MAX_RETRIES=3
# PI=3.14                      # ❌ Error: cannot modify readonly

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SPECIAL VARIABLES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

$0        # Script name
$1 - $9   # Positional parameters (arguments)
$#        # Number of arguments
$@        # All arguments (as separate words)
$*        # All arguments (as single string)
$$        # Current process ID (PID)
$?        # Exit code of last command
$!        # PID of last background command

# Example script using arguments
cat > greet.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
echo "Script: $0"
echo "First arg: $1"
echo "Second arg: $2"
echo "All args: $@"
echo "Arg count: $#"
EOF

./greet.sh Alice Bob Charlie
# Script: ./greet.sh
# First arg: Alice
# Second arg: Bob
# All args: Alice Bob Charlie
# Arg count: 3
📋 Special Variables Reference
┌────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Variable   │ Description                                         │
├────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ $0         │ Script name                                         │
│ $1..$9     │ Positional arguments (1st through 9th)              │
│ ${10}+     │ Arguments beyond 9 (need braces)                    │
│ $#         │ Number of arguments                                 │
│ $@         │ All arguments (preserves quoting)                   │
│ $*         │ All arguments (as single string)                    │
│ $$         │ Current process PID                                 │
│ $?         │ Exit code of last command                           │
│ $!         │ PID of last background process                      │
│ $_         │ Last argument of previous command                   │
│ $-         │ Current shell options                               │
├────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES                                            │
├────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ $HOME      │ User's home directory                               │
│ $USER      │ Current username                                    │
│ $PATH      │ Command search path                                 │
│ $PWD       │ Current working directory                           │
│ $HOSTNAME  │ System hostname                                     │
│ $RANDOM    │ Random number (0-32767)                             │
└────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
4.3 Quoting: Single, Double, and Backticks Understand the critical differences in quoting
🔤 Quoting Rules 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    QUOTING COMPARISON                            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   QUOTE TYPE        │ VARIABLES │ SPECIAL CHARS │ USE CASE      │
│   ──────────────────┼───────────┼───────────────┼────────────── │
│   "double quotes"   │ EXPANDED  │ Most escaped  │ Most common   │
│   'single quotes'   │ LITERAL   │ All literal   │ Exact text    │
│   `backticks`       │ N/A       │ N/A           │ Command sub   │
│   $()               │ N/A       │ N/A           │ Command sub   │
│                                                                  │
│   EXAMPLES:                                                      │
│   name="World"                                                   │
│                                                                  │
│   "Hello, $name!"   →  Hello, World!    (expanded)              │
│   'Hello, $name!'   →  Hello, $name!    (literal)               │
│   "Today: $(date)"  →  Today: Mon Jan 15 10:30:00 UTC 2024      │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Quoting Examples
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DOUBLE QUOTES - Variables expand, some escaping
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

name="Alice"
echo "Hello, $name"           # Output: Hello, Alice
echo "Path: $HOME"            # Output: Path: /home/user
echo "Date: $(date +%Y-%m-%d)" # Output: Date: 2024-01-15

# Preserves whitespace
message="Hello    World"
echo "$message"               # Output: Hello    World
echo $message                 # Output: Hello World (word splitting!)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SINGLE QUOTES - Everything literal
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

echo 'Hello, $name'           # Output: Hello, $name (literal $)
echo 'Path: $HOME'            # Output: Path: $HOME (not expanded)
echo 'Use "quotes" inside'    # Output: Use "quotes" inside

# Useful for:
# - Regex patterns
# - Literal dollar signs
# - Search patterns for grep

grep 'ERROR' log.txt          # Search for literal "ERROR"
echo 'Cost: $100'             # Output: Cost: $100

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# MIXING QUOTES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Include quote characters
echo "It's a test"            # Use double for apostrophe
echo 'She said "Hi"'          # Use single for double quotes
echo "She said \"Hi\""        # Escape double quote

# Combine for complex strings
echo "User: $USER says "'$100'" is too much"
# Output: User: alice says $100 is too much

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# COMMON MISTAKES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# MISTAKE: Unquoted variables with spaces
file="my file.txt"
cat $file                     # ❌ Tries: cat my file.txt (2 files!)
cat "$file"                   # ✅ Correct: cat "my file.txt"

# MISTAKE: Wrong quotes for variables
name="Bob"
echo 'Hello, $name'           # ❌ Output: Hello, $name
echo "Hello, $name"           # ✅ Output: Hello, Bob

# RULE OF THUMB: Always quote variables!
# "$variable" is almost always correct
4.7 Exit Codes and Script Status Handle success and failure in scripts
📊 Exit Code Convention 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    EXIT CODE MEANINGS                            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   CODE  │ MEANING                                                │
│   ──────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────── │
│   0     │ ✅ Success (everything worked)                        │
│   1     │ ❌ General error                                       │
│   2     │ ❌ Misuse of shell command / invalid args              │
│   126   │ ❌ Command found but not executable                    │
│   127   │ ❌ Command not found                                   │
│   128+N │ ❌ Fatal signal N (e.g., 130 = Ctrl+C/SIGINT)          │
│   130   │ ❌ Interrupted (Ctrl+C)                                │
│   255   │ ❌ Exit code out of range                              │
│                                                                  │
│   BEST PRACTICE: Use 0 for success, 1 for general errors        │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Using Exit Codes
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CHECKING EXIT CODES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Check with $?
ls /tmp
echo "Exit code: $?"           # Output: 0 (success)

ls /nonexistent
echo "Exit code: $?"           # Output: 2 (no such file)

# Inline check with && and ||
mkdir /tmp/test && echo "Success" || echo "Failed"

# Check command success
if command -v python3 > /dev/null; then
    echo "Python3 is installed"
else
    echo "Python3 not found"
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SETTING EXIT CODES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat > deploy.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash

# Exit immediately on error
set -e

# Check prerequisites
if [ ! -f "requirements.txt" ]; then
    echo "❌ Error: requirements.txt not found"
    exit 1
fi

# Check environment
if [ -z "$DATABASE_URL" ]; then
    echo "❌ Error: DATABASE_URL not set"
    exit 2
fi

# Run deployment
echo "Starting deployment..."
# ... deployment commands ...

# Success
echo "✅ Deployment complete"
exit 0
EOF

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DEVOPS SCRIPT WITH PROPER ERROR HANDLING
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat > healthcheck.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

URL="${1:-http://localhost:8000/health/}"

# Check if service responds
response=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$URL" || true)

case $response in
    200)
        echo "✅ Service healthy"
        exit 0
        ;;
    5*)
        echo "❌ Server error: $response"
        exit 1
        ;;
    *)
        echo "⚠️ Unexpected response: $response"
        exit 2
        ;;
esac
EOF

chmod +x healthcheck.sh
./healthcheck.sh; echo "Exit: $?"
4.9 Script Templates and Boilerplate Professional script structure for DevOps
📄 devops_script_template.sh Template 
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SCRIPT: script_name.sh
# DESCRIPTION: Brief description of what this script does
# AUTHOR: Your Name <your.email@example.com>
# VERSION: 1.0.0
# USAGE: ./script_name.sh [OPTIONS] ARGUMENTS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STRICT MODE - Catch errors early
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail
# -e: Exit on error
# -u: Error on undefined variables
# -o pipefail: Exit if any command in pipe fails

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# CONSTANTS / CONFIGURATION
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
readonly SCRIPT_NAME=$(basename "$0")
readonly SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)
readonly LOG_FILE="/var/log/${SCRIPT_NAME%.sh}.log"
readonly VERSION="1.0.0"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DEFAULT VALUES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ENVIRONMENT="${ENVIRONMENT:-development}"
VERBOSE="${VERBOSE:-false}"
DRY_RUN="${DRY_RUN:-false}"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# COLORS (for terminal output)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# FUNCTIONS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

log() {
    local level="${1}"
    local message="${2}"
    local timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    echo -e "[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${message}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

info()    { log "INFO" "${GREEN}$1${NC}"; }
warning() { log "WARN" "${YELLOW}$1${NC}"; }
error()   { log "ERROR" "${RED}$1${NC}"; }

die() {
    error "$1"
    exit "${2:-1}"
}

usage() {
    cat << EOF
Usage: ${SCRIPT_NAME} [OPTIONS] <argument>

DESCRIPTION:
    Brief description of script purpose.

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help      Show this help message
    -v, --version   Show version
    -e, --env ENV   Environment (default: development)
    -n, --dry-run   Show what would be done
    --verbose       Enable verbose output

EXAMPLES:
    ${SCRIPT_NAME} --env production deploy
    ${SCRIPT_NAME} -n --verbose deploy

EOF
    exit 0
}

version() {
    echo "${SCRIPT_NAME} version ${VERSION}"
    exit 0
}

cleanup() {
    # Called on EXIT - cleanup resources
    log "INFO" "Cleaning up..."
    # Add cleanup tasks here
}

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ARGUMENT PARSING
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

parse_args() {
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            -h|--help)    usage ;;
            -v|--version) version ;;
            -e|--env)     ENVIRONMENT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
            -n|--dry-run) DRY_RUN=true; shift ;;
            --verbose)    VERBOSE=true; shift ;;
            -*)           die "Unknown option: $1" 2 ;;
            *)            COMMAND="$1"; shift; break ;;
        esac
