23.7 Patch Management Scripts
23.2 Package Management Automation Cross-platform package management
📦 Package Managers by Platform 
┌─────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Platform        │ Package Manager         │ Config Location       │
├─────────────────┼─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤
│ Ubuntu/Debian   │ apt                     │ /etc/apt/             │
│ CentOS/RHEL     │ dnf (yum legacy)        │ /etc/yum.repos.d/     │
│ Alpine          │ apk                     │ /etc/apk/             │
│ macOS           │ brew                    │ Brewfile              │
│ Windows         │ winget / choco          │ manifest file         │
│ Python          │ pip                     │ requirements.txt      │
│ Node.js         │ npm / pnpm / yarn       │ package.json          │
└─────────────────┴─────────────────────────┴───────────────────────┘
▶️ Cross-Platform Package Installation Script
#!/bin/bash
# install-packages.sh - Cross-platform package installation

set -euo pipefail

# Detect OS
detect_os() {
    if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
        . /etc/os-release
        echo "$ID"
    elif [[ "$(uname)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
        echo "macos"
    else
        echo "unknown"
    fi
}

# Package installation by OS
install_packages() {
    local os=$(detect_os)
    
    case "$os" in
        ubuntu|debian)
            echo "📦 Installing on Debian/Ubuntu..."
            apt-get update
            apt-get install -y \
                curl wget git vim \
                build-essential \
                python3 python3-pip \
                nginx
            ;;
        centos|rhel|fedora)
            echo "📦 Installing on RHEL/CentOS..."
            dnf install -y \
                curl wget git vim \
                gcc gcc-c++ make \
                python3 python3-pip \
                nginx
            ;;
        alpine)
            echo "📦 Installing on Alpine..."
            apk add --no-cache \
                curl wget git vim \
                build-base \
                python3 py3-pip \
                nginx
            ;;
        macos)
            echo "📦 Installing on macOS..."
            brew install \
                curl wget git vim \
                python3 \
                nginx
            ;;
        *)
            echo "❌ Unsupported OS: $os"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
}

install_packages
echo "✅ Packages installed successfully"
23.6 Security Hardening Automation Automated server security baseline
🔒 Security Hardening Checklist 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              SERVER SECURITY HARDENING                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   SSH SECURITY                                                  │
│   □ Disable root login                                          │
│   □ Disable password authentication                             │
│   □ Use key-based authentication only                           │
│   □ Change default port (optional)                              │
│   □ Limit users who can SSH                                     │
│                                                                  │
│   FIREWALL                                                      │
│   □ Enable UFW/firewalld                                        │
│   □ Default deny incoming                                       │
│   □ Allow only necessary ports                                  │
│   □ Rate limit SSH connections                                  │
│                                                                  │
│   SYSTEM                                                        │
│   □ Disable unnecessary services                                │
│   □ Set up automatic security updates                           │
│   □ Configure audit logging                                     │
│   □ Set proper file permissions                                 │
│   □ Remove unnecessary packages                                 │
│                                                                  │
│   USERS                                                         │
│   □ Strong password policy                                      │
│   □ Remove default/test accounts                                │
│   □ Configure sudo properly                                     │
│   □ Set up MFA where possible                                   │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Security Hardening Script (Ubuntu/Debian)
#!/bin/bash
# harden-server.sh - Security hardening for Ubuntu/Debian

set -euo pipefail

echo "🔒 Starting security hardening..."

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SSH HARDENING
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

backup_file() {
    [[ -f "$1" ]] && cp "$1" "${1}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d)"
}

harden_ssh() {
    echo "🔐 Hardening SSH..."
    backup_file /etc/ssh/sshd_config
    
    cat >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/hardening.conf <<'EOF'
# Security hardening
PermitRootLogin no
PasswordAuthentication no
PubkeyAuthentication yes
PermitEmptyPasswords no
X11Forwarding no
MaxAuthTries 3
ClientAliveInterval 300
ClientAliveCountMax 2
AllowAgentForwarding no
AllowTcpForwarding no
EOF

    systemctl restart sshd
}

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FIREWALL (UFW)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

setup_firewall() {
    echo "🧱 Configuring firewall..."
    ufw --force reset
    ufw default deny incoming
    ufw default allow outgoing
    ufw allow 22/tcp comment 'SSH'
    ufw allow 80/tcp comment 'HTTP'
    ufw allow 443/tcp comment 'HTTPS'
    ufw --force enable
}

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# AUTOMATIC UPDATES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

setup_auto_updates() {
    echo "📦 Configuring automatic security updates..."
    apt-get install -y unattended-upgrades
    
    cat > /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/20auto-upgrades <<EOF
APT::Periodic::Update-Package-Lists "1";
APT::Periodic::Unattended-Upgrade "1";
APT::Periodic::AutocleanInterval "7";
EOF
}

# Run all hardening functions
harden_ssh
setup_firewall
setup_auto_updates

echo "✅ Security hardening complete"

Chapter 24: Application Configuration

Managing application-level configuration

Chapter Topics

24.1 Environment-Specific Configurations
24.2 Configuration File Generation (Jinja2 Templates)
24.3 Secret Injection Patterns
24.4 Feature Flags Automation
24.5 Database Configuration
24.6 Cache and Queue Configuration
24.7 Configuration Validation Scripts
24.2 Configuration File Generation (Jinja2 Templates) Dynamic config file generation
📄 Jinja2 Template Syntax 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              JINJA2 QUICK REFERENCE                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   VARIABLES:                                                    │
│   {{ variable }}                 Output variable value          │
│   {{ user.name }}               Access object property          │
│   {{ items[0] }}                Access list item                │
│                                                                  │
│   FILTERS:                                                      │
│   {{ name | upper }}            Uppercase                       │
│   {{ name | default('N/A') }}   Default value                   │
│   {{ items | join(',') }}       Join list                       │
│   {{ text | truncate(50) }}     Truncate string                 │
│                                                                  │
│   CONTROL FLOW:                                                 │
│   {% if condition %}            Conditional                     │
│   {% elif other %}                                              │
│   {% else %}                                                    │
│   {% endif %}                                                   │
│                                                                  │
│   {% for item in items %}       Loop                            │
│   {{ item }}                                                    │
│   {% endfor %}                                                  │
│                                                                  │
│   COMMENTS:                                                     │
│   {# This is a comment #}                                       │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Python Config Generator with Jinja2
"""
Configuration file generator using Jinja2
pip install jinja2 pyyaml
"""
from pathlib import Path
from jinja2 import Environment, FileSystemLoader
import yaml

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CONFIG GENERATOR CLASS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class ConfigGenerator:
    """Generate configuration files from Jinja2 templates."""
    
    def __init__(self, templates_dir: str = "templates"):
        self.env = Environment(
            loader=FileSystemLoader(templates_dir),
            trim_blocks=True,
            lstrip_blocks=True
        )
    
    def render(self, template_name: str, **variables) -> str:
        """Render a template with given variables."""
        template = self.env.get_template(template_name)
        return template.render(**variables)
    
    def write(self, template_name: str, output_path: str, **variables):
        """Render template and write to file."""
        content = self.render(template_name, **variables)
        Path(output_path).write_text(content)
        print(f"✅ Generated: {output_path}")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# USAGE EXAMPLE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Load environment-specific variables
def load_env_config(env: str) -> dict:
    with open(f"config/{env}.yaml") as f:
        return yaml.safe_load(f)

# Generate nginx config
generator = ConfigGenerator("templates")
env_config = load_env_config("production")

generator.write(
    "nginx.conf.j2",
    "/etc/nginx/sites-available/app",
    **env_config
)
▶️ Example Template: nginx.conf.j2
# templates/nginx.conf.j2
# Generated by ConfigGenerator - Do not edit manually

upstream {{ app_name }}_backend {
{% for server in backend_servers %}
    server {{ server.host }}:{{ server.port }} weight={{ server.weight | default(1) }};
{% endfor %}
}

server {
    listen {{ listen_port | default(80) }};
    server_name {{ domain }};

{% if ssl_enabled %}
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    ssl_certificate /etc/ssl/{{ domain }}.crt;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/ssl/{{ domain }}.key;
{% endif %}

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://{{ app_name }}_backend;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
{% if enable_websockets %}
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
{% endif %}
    }

{% for location in custom_locations | default([]) %}
    location {{ location.path }} {
        {{ location.config }}
    }
{% endfor %}
}
24.3 Secret Injection Patterns Securely handling sensitive configuration
🔐 Secret Injection Methods 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              SECRET INJECTION PATTERNS                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   METHOD              │ SECURITY │ COMPLEXITY │ BEST FOR        │
│   ────────────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼────────────────  │
│   Environment Vars    │ Medium   │ Low        │ Simple apps     │
│   Mounted Files       │ Medium   │ Low        │ K8s, containers │
│   Vault (HashiCorp)   │ High     │ Medium     │ Enterprise      │
│   AWS Secrets Manager │ High     │ Medium     │ AWS apps        │
│   Azure Key Vault     │ High     │ Medium     │ Azure apps      │
│   SOPS (encrypted)    │ High     │ Medium     │ GitOps          │
│   1Password CLI       │ High     │ Low        │ Dev teams       │
│                                                                  │
│   NEVER DO:                                                     │
│   ✗ Secrets in code (git history!)                              │
│   ✗ Secrets in Docker images                                    │
│   ✗ Plain text config files in repos                            │
│   ✗ Logging secrets accidentally                                │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Core concepts of continuous integration and deployment

Chapter Topics

25.1 CI/CD Concepts and Benefits
25.2 Pipeline Architecture Patterns

25.4 Environment Promotion Strategies
25.5 Artifact Management

25.1 CI/CD Concepts and Benefits Understanding the CI/CD pipeline
🔄 CI/CD Pipeline Flow 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              CI/CD PIPELINE ARCHITECTURE                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   CONTINUOUS INTEGRATION (CI)                                   │
│   ┌─────────┐   ┌─────────┐   ┌─────────┐   ┌─────────┐        │
│   │  Code   │──►│  Build  │──►│  Test   │──►│ Analyze │        │
│   │  Push   │   │         │   │         │   │ (SAST)  │        │
│   └─────────┘   └─────────┘   └─────────┘   └─────────┘        │
│                                                                  │
│   CONTINUOUS DELIVERY (CD)                                      │
│   ┌─────────┐   ┌─────────┐   ┌─────────┐   ┌─────────┐        │
│   │ Package │──►│  Dev    │──►│ Staging │──►│  Prod   │        │
│   │Artifact │   │ Deploy  │   │ Deploy  │   │ (Manual)│        │
│   └─────────┘   └─────────┘   └─────────┘   └─────────┘        │
│                                                 ▲                │
│   CONTINUOUS DEPLOYMENT                         │                │
│   (Same as above, but Prod deploy is automated)─┘                │
│                                                                  │
│   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════   │
│                                                                  │
│   CI vs CD vs CD:                                               │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │ CI (Integration)  │ Every commit builds and tests       │   │
│   │ CD (Delivery)     │ Every commit is deployable          │   │
│   │ CD (Deployment)   │ Every commit auto-deploys to prod   │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
25.4 Environment Promotion Strategies Moving code through environments
🚀 Deployment Strategies 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              DEPLOYMENT STRATEGIES                               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   ROLLING UPDATE                                                │
│   ┌─────┐  ┌─────┐  ┌─────┐  ┌─────┐                           │
│   │ v1  │  │ v1  │  │ v1  │  │ v1  │   Initial                 │
│   └─────┘  └─────┘  └─────┘  └─────┘                           │
│   ┌─────┐  ┌─────┐  ┌─────┐  ┌─────┐                           │
│   │ v2  │  │ v1  │  │ v1  │  │ v1  │   25% updated             │
│   └─────┘  └─────┘  └─────┘  └─────┘                           │
│   ┌─────┐  ┌─────┐  ┌─────┐  ┌─────┐                           │
│   │ v2  │  │ v2  │  │ v2  │  │ v2  │   Complete                │
│   └─────┘  └─────┘  └─────┘  └─────┘                           │
│                                                                  │
│   BLUE-GREEN                                                    │
│   ┌────────────┐        ┌────────────┐                         │
│   │ BLUE (v1)  │◄──LB   │ GREEN (v2) │  Deploy to Green        │
│   └────────────┘        └────────────┘                         │
│   ┌────────────┐        ┌────────────┐                         │
│   │ BLUE (v1)  │    LB──►│ GREEN (v2) │  Switch traffic        │
│   └────────────┘        └────────────┘                         │
│                                                                  │
│   CANARY                                                        │
│   ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐                   │
│   │        Production (v1) - 95%           │                   │
│   └────────────────────────────────────────┘                   │
│   ┌──────┐                                                      │
│   │v2 5% │  Test v2 with small traffic %                       │
│   └──────┘                                                      │
│                                                                  │
│   ┌──────────────────────────────────┐                         │
│   │    Production (v2) - 50%         │  Gradually increase     │
│   └──────────────────────────────────┘                         │
│   ┌──────────────────────────────────┐                         │
│   │    Production (v1) - 50%         │                         │
│   └──────────────────────────────────┘                         │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Automating workflows with GitHub Actions

26.1 Workflow Files (.yml) GitHub Actions workflow anatomy
📁 Workflow File Structure 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              GITHUB ACTIONS HIERARCHY                            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   .github/                                                      │
│   └── workflows/                                                │
│       ├── ci.yml          # CI workflow                         │
│       ├── cd.yml          # CD workflow                         │
│       └── pr-check.yml    # PR validation                       │
│                                                                  │
│   WORKFLOW STRUCTURE:                                           │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │ Workflow (.yml file)                                    │   │
│   │ ├── name: Workflow name                                 │   │
│   │ ├── on: triggers                                        │   │
│   │ └── jobs:                                               │   │
│   │     ├── job_1:                                          │   │
│   │     │   ├── runs-on: runner                             │   │
│   │     │   ├── needs: [dependencies]                       │   │
│   │     │   └── steps:                                      │   │
│   │     │       ├── - uses: action@version                  │   │
│   │     │       └── - run: command                          │   │
│   │     └── job_2:                                          │   │
│   │         └── ...                                         │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Complete CI/CD Workflow Example
# .github/workflows/ci-cd.yml
name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:  # Manual trigger

env:
  NODE_VERSION: '20'
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: ${{ github.repository }}

jobs:
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # BUILD AND TEST
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'npm'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
      
      - name: Run linting
        run: npm run lint
      
      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test -- --coverage
      
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build
      
      - name: Upload build artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build
          path: dist/
          retention-days: 7

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # SECURITY SCANNING
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  security:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
      
      - name: Upload to GitHub Security
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        with:
          sarif_file: 'trivy-results.sarif'

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # DOCKER BUILD
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  docker:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build, security]
    if: github.event_name == 'push'
    
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Download build artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build
          path: dist/
      
      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Build and push Docker image
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          push: true
          tags: |
            ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:${{ github.sha }}
            ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:latest

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # DEPLOY TO PRODUCTION
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: docker
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment: production  # Requires approval
    
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to production
        run: |
          echo "Deploying ${{ github.sha }} to production"
          # Add your deployment commands here
26.2 Triggers and Events When workflows run
⚡ GitHub Actions Trigger Events 
┌──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Trigger              │ When It Fires                             │
├──────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ push                 │ Code pushed to branch                     │
│ pull_request         │ PR opened, synced, reopened              │
│ pull_request_target  │ PR from fork (careful with secrets!)     │
│ workflow_dispatch    │ Manual trigger with inputs               │
│ schedule             │ Cron schedule                            │
│ release              │ Release published/created                │
│ workflow_call        │ Called by another workflow               │
│ repository_dispatch  │ External webhook                         │
│ issues               │ Issue opened/closed/labeled              │
│ issue_comment        │ Comment on issue or PR                   │
└──────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Advanced Trigger Examples
on:
  # Push to specific branches
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - 'release/**'
    paths:
      - 'src/**'
      - '!src/**/*.md'  # Ignore markdown changes
    tags:
      - 'v*.*.*'

  # Pull request events
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
    branches: [main]

  # Scheduled (cron)
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * 1-5'  # Mon-Fri at 2 AM UTC

  # Manual trigger with inputs
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      environment:
        description: 'Deploy environment'
        required: true
        default: 'staging'
        type: choice
        options:
          - staging
          - production
      dry_run:
        description: 'Perform dry run'
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
26.7 Matrix Builds Test across multiple configurations
▶️ Matrix Strategy Examples
# Test across multiple Node.js versions and OS
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false  # Continue other jobs if one fails
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]
        node: [18, 20, 22]
        # Exclude specific combinations
        exclude:
          - os: macos-latest
            node: 18
        # Add specific combinations
        include:
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            node: 20
            experimental: true
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Setup Node ${{ matrix.node }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test
        continue-on-error: ${{ matrix.experimental || false }}

Chapter 27: Jenkins Automation

Jenkins pipelines and automation

Chapter Topics

27.1 Jenkins Pipeline (Declarative & Scripted)
27.2 Jenkinsfile Best Practices
27.3 Shared Libraries
27.4 Jenkins Configuration as Code (JCasC)
27.5 Agent Management
27.6 Plugin Automation
27.7 Jenkins API Scripting
27.1 Jenkins Pipeline Types Declarative vs Scripted pipelines
📋 Declarative vs Scripted Pipeline 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              JENKINS PIPELINE TYPES                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   DECLARATIVE PIPELINE           SCRIPTED PIPELINE              │
│   ───────────────────            ─────────────────              │
│   • Structured syntax            • Full Groovy power            │
│   • Easier to read               • More flexibility             │
│   • Validation at start          • Complex logic possible       │
│   • Best for most cases          • Advanced use cases           │
│                                                                  │
│   pipeline {                     node {                         │
│     agent any                      stage('Build') {             │
│     stages {                         sh 'make'                  │
│       stage('Build') {             }                            │
│         steps {                    stage('Test') {              │
│           sh 'make'                  try {                      │
│         }                              sh 'make test'           │
│       }                              } catch (e) {               │
│     }                                  currentBuild.result = .. │
│   }                                  }                           │
│                                    }                             │
│                                  }                               │
│                                                                  │
│   RECOMMENDATION: Start with Declarative, use Scripted         │
│   blocks (script { }) only when needed.                         │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Complete Jenkinsfile Example
// Jenkinsfile - Declarative Pipeline
pipeline {
    agent {
        docker {
            image 'node:20-alpine'
            args '-v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock'
        }
    }
    
    environment {
        CI = 'true'
        DOCKER_REGISTRY = 'registry.example.com'
        APP_NAME = 'anihpj-app'
    }
    
    options {
        buildDiscarder(logRotator(numToKeepStr: '10'))
        timeout(time: 30, unit: 'MINUTES')
        disableConcurrentBuilds()
    }
    
    stages {
        stage('Checkout') {
            steps {
                checkout scm
            }
        }
        
        stage('Install') {
            steps {
                sh 'npm ci'
            }
        }
        
        stage('Lint & Test') {
            parallel {
                stage('Lint') {
                    steps {
                        sh 'npm run lint'
                    }
                }
                stage('Unit Tests') {
                    steps {
                        sh 'npm test -- --coverage'
                    }
                    post {
                        always {
                            publishHTML([
                                reportDir: 'coverage',
                                reportFiles: 'index.html',
                                reportName: 'Coverage Report'
                            ])
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                sh 'npm run build'
            }
        }
        
        stage('Docker Build') {
            when {
                branch 'main'
            }
            steps {
                script {
                    def image = docker.build("${DOCKER_REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:${BUILD_NUMBER}")
                    docker.withRegistry("https://${DOCKER_REGISTRY}", 'docker-creds') {
                        image.push()
                        image.push('latest')
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        
        stage('Deploy to Staging') {
            when {
                branch 'main'
            }
            steps {
                sh './deploy.sh staging'
            }
        }
        
        stage('Deploy to Production') {
            when {
                branch 'main'
            }
            input {
                message 'Deploy to production?'
                ok 'Deploy'
            }
            steps {
                sh './deploy.sh production'
            }
        }
    }
    
    post {
        always {
            cleanWs()
        }
        success {
            slackSend(color: 'good', message: "✅ Build ${BUILD_NUMBER} succeeded")
        }
        failure {
            slackSend(color: 'danger', message: "❌ Build ${BUILD_NUMBER} failed")
        }
    }
}

Chapter 28: Advanced GitHub Actions

Advanced workflow patterns and automation with GitHub Actions

Chapter Topics

28.1 Workflow File Structure
28.2 Jobs and Steps
28.3 GitHub-Hosted Runners
28.4 Artifacts and Caching
28.5 Environments and Deployments
28.6 Reusable Workflows
28.7 GitHub API Automation
28.4 Artifacts and Caching Build optimization strategies
📋 Caching vs Artifacts Comparison
FeatureCacheArtifacts
PurposeSpeed up workflowsShare files between jobs/runs
ScopeAcross workflow runsWithin a workflow run
Typical UseDependencies (node_modules)Build outputs, test results
Retention7 days (or branch deletion)90 days (configurable)
Size Limit10 GB per repoVaries by plan
▶️ Complete Caching & Artifacts Example
# .github/workflows/optimized-build.yml
name: Optimized Build with Caching

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      artifact-path: ${{ steps.upload.outputs.artifact-path }}
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      # Node.js dependency caching
      - name: Setup Node.js with cache
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
      
      # Custom cache for build outputs
      - name: Cache Webpack build
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        id: webpack-cache
        with:
          path: |
            .next/cache
            node_modules/.cache
          key: webpack-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}-${{ hashFiles('src/**') }}
          restore-keys: |
            webpack-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}-
            webpack-${{ runner.os }}-
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
      
      - name: Build
        run: npm run build
      
      # Upload build artifacts for other jobs
      - name: Upload build artifacts
        id: upload
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-output
          path: |
            dist/
            .next/
          retention-days: 5
  
  deploy:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      # Download artifacts from build job
      - name: Download build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-output
          path: ./deploy
      
      - name: Deploy
        run: |
          ls -la ./deploy
          echo "Deploying artifacts..."
▶️ Multi-Platform Cache Example
# Cache configuration per language/tool
caching_examples:
  python:
    - name: Cache pip dependencies
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: ~/.cache/pip
        key: pip-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
  
  go:
    - name: Cache Go modules
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: |
          ~/go/pkg/mod
          ~/.cache/go-build
        key: go-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/go.sum') }}
  
  gradle:
    - name: Cache Gradle packages
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: |
          ~/.gradle/caches
          ~/.gradle/wrapper
        key: gradle-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/*.gradle*', '**/gradle-wrapper.properties') }}
  
  docker:
    - name: Cache Docker layers
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: /tmp/.buildx-cache
        key: docker-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/Dockerfile') }}
        restore-keys: docker-${{ runner.os }}-
28.6 Reusable Workflows DRY workflow patterns
▶️ Reusable Workflow Definition
# .github/workflows/reusable-docker.yml
name: Reusable Docker Build

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      image-name:
        required: true
        type: string
        description: 'Docker image name'
      context:
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '.'
        description: 'Build context path'
      dockerfile:
        required: false
        type: string
        default: 'Dockerfile'
      push:
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
    secrets:
      registry-username:
        required: true
      registry-password:
        required: true
    outputs:
      image-tag:
        description: 'Full image tag'
        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.tag }}

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Docker meta
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ inputs.image-name }}
          tags: |
            type=sha
            type=ref,event=branch
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
      
      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      
      - name: Login to Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          username: ${{ secrets.registry-username }}
          password: ${{ secrets.registry-password }}
      
      - name: Build and push
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: ${{ inputs.context }}
          file: ${{ inputs.dockerfile }}
          push: ${{ inputs.push }}
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
▶️ Calling Reusable Workflows
# .github/workflows/ci.yml - Calling the reusable workflow
name: CI Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build-api:
    uses: ./.github/workflows/reusable-docker.yml
    with:
      image-name: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}/api
      context: ./services/api
      push: true
    secrets:
      registry-username: ${{ github.actor }}
      registry-password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
  
  build-web:
    uses: ./.github/workflows/reusable-docker.yml
    with:
      image-name: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}/web
      context: ./services/web
      push: true
    secrets:
      registry-username: ${{ github.actor }}
      registry-password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
  
  deploy:
    needs: [build-api, build-web]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy using built images
        run: |
          echo "API: ${{ needs.build-api.outputs.image-tag }}"
          echo "Web: ${{ needs.build-web.outputs.image-tag }}"

29.1 Build Scripts and Makefiles Universal build orchestration
📋 Build Automation Overview 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              BUILD AUTOMATION TOOLS                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   MAKE (Universal)         │  Language-Specific                 │
│   ─────────────────        │  ────────────────                  │
│   • Cross-platform         │  npm scripts (Node.js)             │
│   • Dependency tracking    │  pip/poetry (Python)               │
│   • Parallel builds        │  gradle/maven (Java)               │
│   • Shell integration      │  cargo (Rust)                      │
│                            │  go build (Go)                     │
│                                                                  │
│   BUILD PROCESS FLOW                                            │
│   ──────────────────                                            │
│                                                                  │
│   Source ──▶ Compile ──▶ Test ──▶ Package ──▶ Publish          │
│     │          │          │         │           │               │
│     ▼          ▼          ▼         ▼           ▼               │
│   [lint]    [build]    [unit]   [docker]   [registry]          │
│             [deps]     [integ]   [helm]      [npm]             │
│                        [e2e]    [artifact]   [pypi]            │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Universal Project Makefile
# Makefile - Universal build orchestration
.PHONY: all build test lint clean docker deploy help

# Variables
APP_NAME := myapp
VERSION := $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty)
BUILD_TIME := $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
DOCKER_REGISTRY := ghcr.io/myorg
DOCKER_IMAGE := $(DOCKER_REGISTRY)/$(APP_NAME)

# Go build flags
LDFLAGS := -ldflags "-X main.Version=$(VERSION) -X main.BuildTime=$(BUILD_TIME)"

# Default target
all: lint test build

## help: Show this help message
help:
	@echo "Available targets:"
	@grep -E '^## ' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## /  /'

## deps: Install dependencies
deps:
	go mod download
	go mod verify

## lint: Run linters
lint:
	golangci-lint run ./...
	go vet ./...

## test: Run tests with coverage
test:
	go test -v -race -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...
	go tool cover -html=coverage.out -o coverage.html

## build: Build the application
build:
	CGO_ENABLED=0 go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/$(APP_NAME) ./cmd/$(APP_NAME)

## build-all: Build for all platforms
build-all:
	GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/$(APP_NAME)-linux-amd64 ./cmd/$(APP_NAME)
	GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/$(APP_NAME)-linux-arm64 ./cmd/$(APP_NAME)
	GOOS=darwin GOARCH=amd64 go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/$(APP_NAME)-darwin-amd64 ./cmd/$(APP_NAME)
	GOOS=darwin GOARCH=arm64 go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/$(APP_NAME)-darwin-arm64 ./cmd/$(APP_NAME)
	GOOS=windows GOARCH=amd64 go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/$(APP_NAME)-windows-amd64.exe ./cmd/$(APP_NAME)

## docker: Build Docker image
docker:
	docker build -t $(DOCKER_IMAGE):$(VERSION) -t $(DOCKER_IMAGE):latest \
		--build-arg VERSION=$(VERSION) \
		--build-arg BUILD_TIME=$(BUILD_TIME) .

## docker-push: Push Docker image
docker-push: docker
	docker push $(DOCKER_IMAGE):$(VERSION)
	docker push $(DOCKER_IMAGE):latest

## clean: Clean build artifacts
clean:
	rm -rf bin/ coverage.out coverage.html
	go clean -cache -testcache

## dev: Run with hot reload
dev:
	air -c .air.toml

## run: Run the application
run: build
	./bin/$(APP_NAME)
29.2 Docker Build Automation Optimized multi-stage builds
▶️ Optimized Multi-Stage Dockerfile
# Dockerfile - Optimized multi-stage build
# Stage 1: Dependencies
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --only=production && \
    cp -R node_modules /prod_modules && \
    npm ci

# Stage 2: Build
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# Stage 3: Production
FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app

# Security: non-root user
RUN addgroup -g 1001 -S nodejs && \
    adduser -S nextjs -u 1001

# Production dependencies only
COPY --from=deps /prod_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./

# Metadata
ARG VERSION=unknown
ARG BUILD_TIME=unknown
LABEL version="${VERSION}" \
      build-time="${BUILD_TIME}" \
      maintainer="team@example.com"

ENV NODE_ENV=production
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]
▶️ Docker Build Script
#!/bin/bash
# build-docker.sh - Automated Docker build with caching

set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
REGISTRY="${DOCKER_REGISTRY:-ghcr.io/myorg}"
IMAGE_NAME="${IMAGE_NAME:-myapp}"
DOCKERFILE="${DOCKERFILE:-Dockerfile}"
CONTEXT="${CONTEXT:-.}"

# Get version from git
VERSION=$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo "dev")
BUILD_TIME=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
COMMIT_SHA=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")

# Full image reference
FULL_IMAGE="${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME}"

echo "Building ${FULL_IMAGE}:${VERSION}"

# Build with BuildKit for better caching
DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build \
    --file "${DOCKERFILE}" \
    --build-arg VERSION="${VERSION}" \
    --build-arg BUILD_TIME="${BUILD_TIME}" \
    --build-arg COMMIT_SHA="${COMMIT_SHA}" \
    --cache-from "${FULL_IMAGE}:cache" \
    --tag "${FULL_IMAGE}:${VERSION}" \
    --tag "${FULL_IMAGE}:${COMMIT_SHA}" \
    --tag "${FULL_IMAGE}:latest" \
    "${CONTEXT}"

# Push if requested
if [[ "${PUSH:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
    echo "Pushing images..."
    docker push "${FULL_IMAGE}:${VERSION}"
    docker push "${FULL_IMAGE}:${COMMIT_SHA}"
    docker push "${FULL_IMAGE}:latest"
fi

echo "Build complete: ${FULL_IMAGE}:${VERSION}"
29.3 Multi-Architecture Builds Cross-platform container images
📋 Multi-Arch Build Matrix
PlatformUse CaseCommon Targets
linux/amd64Standard x86 serversAWS EC2, GCP, Azure
linux/arm64ARM servers, AWS GravitonAWS Graviton, Apple M1/M2
linux/arm/v7Raspberry Pi, IoTEdge devices
▶️ Multi-Arch Build Script
#!/bin/bash
# build-multiarch.sh - Build multi-architecture images

set -euo pipefail

IMAGE="${1:-myapp}"
VERSION="${2:-latest}"
PLATFORMS="${PLATFORMS:-linux/amd64,linux/arm64}"

# Create and use buildx builder
docker buildx create --name multiarch --use 2>/dev/null || docker buildx use multiarch
docker buildx inspect --bootstrap

# Build and push multi-arch image
docker buildx build \
    --platform "${PLATFORMS}" \
    --tag "${IMAGE}:${VERSION}" \
    --tag "${IMAGE}:latest" \
    --push \
    --cache-from "type=registry,ref=${IMAGE}:buildcache" \
    --cache-to "type=registry,ref=${IMAGE}:buildcache,mode=max" \
    .

echo "Multi-arch build complete for platforms: ${PLATFORMS}"

# Inspect manifest
docker buildx imagetools inspect "${IMAGE}:${VERSION}"
29.6 Artifact Versioning Semantic versioning automation
▶️ Automated Version Management
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""version_manager.py - Semantic version management"""

import re
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime

class VersionManager:
    """Manage semantic versioning for projects."""
    
    def __init__(self, version_file: str = "VERSION"):
        self.version_file = Path(version_file)
        
    def get_current_version(self) -> str:
        """Get current version from file or git tags."""
        if self.version_file.exists():
            return self.version_file.read_text().strip()
        
        # Try git describe
        try:
            result = subprocess.run(
                ["git", "describe", "--tags", "--abbrev=0"],
                capture_output=True, text=True, check=True
            )
            return result.stdout.strip().lstrip("v")
        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
            return "0.0.0"
    
    def bump_version(self, bump_type: str = "patch") -> str:
        """Bump version: major, minor, or patch."""
        current = self.get_current_version()
        match = re.match(r"(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)", current)
        
        if not match:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid version format: {current}")
        
        major, minor, patch = map(int, match.groups())
        
        if bump_type == "major":
            major += 1
            minor = 0
            patch = 0
        elif bump_type == "minor":
            minor += 1
            patch = 0
        elif bump_type == "patch":
            patch += 1
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid bump type: {bump_type}")
        
        new_version = f"{major}.{minor}.{patch}"
        self.version_file.write_text(new_version)
        return new_version
    
    def get_build_info(self) -> dict:
        """Get comprehensive build metadata."""
        try:
            commit = subprocess.run(
                ["git", "rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD"],
                capture_output=True, text=True, check=True
            ).stdout.strip()
            
            branch = subprocess.run(
                ["git", "rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "HEAD"],
                capture_output=True, text=True, check=True
            ).stdout.strip()
        except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
            commit = "unknown"
            branch = "unknown"
        
        return {
            "version": self.get_current_version(),
            "commit": commit,
            "branch": branch,
            "build_time": datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
            "build_number": os.environ.get("BUILD_NUMBER", "local")
        }

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import sys
    import os
    
    vm = VersionManager()
    
    if len(sys.argv) > 1:
        action = sys.argv[1]
        if action == "bump":
            bump_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "patch"
            new_ver = vm.bump_version(bump_type)
            print(f"Version bumped to: {new_ver}")
        elif action == "info":
            import json
            print(json.dumps(vm.get_build_info(), indent=2))
    else:
        print(vm.get_current_version())

Chapter 30: Deployment Automation

Automating application deployments

Chapter Topics

30.1 Deployment Strategies (Rolling, Blue-Green, Canary)
30.2 Zero-Downtime Deployments
30.3 Database Migration Automation
30.4 Rollback Scripts
30.5 Smoke Test Automation
30.6 Feature Toggle Deployment
30.7 Multi-Environment Deployment Scripts
30.1 Deployment Strategies Rolling, Blue-Green, and Canary
📋 Deployment Strategy Comparison 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              DEPLOYMENT STRATEGIES                               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│ ROLLING                  BLUE-GREEN              CANARY          │
│ ───────                  ──────────              ──────          │
│ ┌─┬─┬─┬─┐               ┌─────────┐             ┌────┐ 95%      │
│ │●│●│○│○│  Gradual      │  BLUE   │◀── Live    │OLD │───▶ Users│
│ └─┴─┴─┴─┘  replacement  ├─────────┤             ├────┤          │
│                         │ GREEN   │◀── Standby │NEW │───▶ 5%   │
│ Low resource            └─────────┘             └────┘          │
│ Fast rollback            Instant switch          Gradual test   │
│                         Zero downtime            Low risk        │
│                                                                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Strategy    │ Downtime │ Rollback │ Resource │ Risk │ Complexity│
├─────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────┼───────────│
│ Rolling     │ Minimal  │ Slow     │ Low      │ Med  │ Low       │
│ Blue-Green  │ Zero     │ Instant  │ High     │ Low  │ Medium    │
│ Canary      │ Zero     │ Fast     │ Medium   │ Low  │ High      │
│ Recreate    │ Yes      │ N/A      │ Low      │ High │ Low       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Blue-Green Deployment Script
#!/bin/bash
# deploy-blue-green.sh - Blue-Green deployment automation

set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
APP_NAME="${APP_NAME:-myapp}"
IMAGE="${IMAGE:-myapp:latest}"
HEALTH_ENDPOINT="${HEALTH_ENDPOINT:-/health}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-300}"

# Detect current active environment
get_active_env() {
    # Returns 'blue' or 'green' based on current target group
    aws elbv2 describe-target-groups \
        --names "${APP_NAME}-tg" \
        --query 'TargetGroups[0].Tags[?Key==`Environment`].Value' \
        --output text 2>/dev/null || echo "blue"
}

# Wait for service health
wait_for_health() {
    local endpoint="$1"
    local timeout="$2"
    local start
    start=$(date +%s)
    
    echo "Waiting for health check at ${endpoint}..."
    
    while true; do
        if curl -sf "${endpoint}${HEALTH_ENDPOINT}" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "✓ Service is healthy"
            return 0
        fi
        
        local elapsed=$(( $(date +%s) - start ))
        if [[ $elapsed -ge $timeout ]]; then
            echo "✗ Health check timeout after ${timeout}s"
            return 1
        fi
        
        sleep 5
    done
}

# Main deployment
deploy() {
    local active_env
    local target_env
    
    active_env=$(get_active_env)
    
    if [[ "$active_env" == "blue" ]]; then
        target_env="green"
    else
        target_env="blue"
    fi
    
    echo "Current: ${active_env}, Deploying to: ${target_env}"
    
    # Deploy to target environment
    echo "Step 1: Deploying ${IMAGE} to ${target_env}..."
    kubectl set image deployment/${APP_NAME}-${target_env} \
        app=${IMAGE} --record
    
    kubectl rollout status deployment/${APP_NAME}-${target_env} \
        --timeout=${TIMEOUT}s
    
    # Run smoke tests
    echo "Step 2: Running smoke tests..."
    TARGET_URL=$(kubectl get svc ${APP_NAME}-${target_env} -o jsonpath='{.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].hostname}')
    
    if ! wait_for_health "http://${TARGET_URL}" "$TIMEOUT"; then
        echo "Smoke tests failed, aborting deployment"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Switch traffic
    echo "Step 3: Switching traffic to ${target_env}..."
    kubectl patch svc ${APP_NAME}-live \
        -p "{\"spec\":{\"selector\":{\"environment\":\"${target_env}\"}}}"
    
    echo "✓ Deployment complete. ${target_env} is now live."
    echo "  Previous version (${active_env}) available for rollback."
}

# Rollback function
rollback() {
    local active_env
    local rollback_env
    
    active_env=$(get_active_env)
    
    if [[ "$active_env" == "blue" ]]; then
        rollback_env="green"
    else
        rollback_env="blue"
    fi
    
    echo "Rolling back from ${active_env} to ${rollback_env}..."
    kubectl patch svc ${APP_NAME}-live \
        -p "{\"spec\":{\"selector\":{\"environment\":\"${rollback_env}\"}}}"
    
    echo "✓ Rollback complete. ${rollback_env} is now live."
}

# Parse arguments
case "${1:-deploy}" in
    deploy)  deploy ;;
    rollback) rollback ;;
    status)  get_active_env ;;
    *)       echo "Usage: $0 {deploy|rollback|status}" && exit 1 ;;
esac
30.3 Database Migration Automation Safe schema migrations
▶️ Database Migration Script
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""db_migrate.py - Automated database migrations with safety checks"""

import os
import sys
import subprocess
import hashlib
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path

class DatabaseMigrator:
    """Safe database migration automation."""
    
    def __init__(self, migrations_dir: str = "migrations"):
        self.migrations_dir = Path(migrations_dir)
        self.db_url = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL")
        
        if not self.db_url:
            raise ValueError("DATABASE_URL environment variable required")
    
    def get_pending_migrations(self) -> list:
        """List migrations not yet applied."""
        applied = self._get_applied_migrations()
        all_migrations = sorted(self.migrations_dir.glob("*.sql"))
        return [m for m in all_migrations if m.name not in applied]
    
    def _get_applied_migrations(self) -> set:
        """Query database for applied migrations."""
        # This would query a migrations tracking table
        # Simplified for demonstration
        return set()
    
    def create_backup(self) -> str:
        """Create database backup before migration."""
        timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
        backup_file = f"backup_{timestamp}.sql"
        
        print(f"Creating backup: {backup_file}")
        
        # PostgreSQL example
        subprocess.run([
            "pg_dump", self.db_url, "-f", backup_file
        ], check=True)
        
        return backup_file
    
    def verify_migration(self, migration_path: Path) -> dict:
        """Verify migration safety checks."""
        content = migration_path.read_text()
        checks = {
            "has_transaction": "BEGIN" in content or "START TRANSACTION" in content,
            "has_rollback": "ROLLBACK" in content or migration_path.with_suffix(".rollback.sql").exists(),
            "no_drop_table": "DROP TABLE" not in content or "IF EXISTS" in content,
            "idempotent": "IF NOT EXISTS" in content or "IF EXISTS" in content,
        }
        return checks
    
    def run_migration(self, migration_path: Path, dry_run: bool = False) -> bool:
        """Execute a single migration."""
        print(f"\n{'[DRY RUN] ' if dry_run else ''}Running: {migration_path.name}")
        
        # Safety verification
        checks = self.verify_migration(migration_path)
        failed_checks = [k for k, v in checks.items() if not v]
        
        if failed_checks:
            print(f"⚠️  Warning: Failed safety checks: {failed_checks}")
            if not dry_run:
                response = input("Continue anyway? [y/N]: ")
                if response.lower() != "y":
                    return False
        
        if dry_run:
            print(f"  Would execute: {migration_path.name}")
            return True
        
        # Execute migration
        try:
            subprocess.run([
                "psql", self.db_url, "-f", str(migration_path)
            ], check=True)
            
            self._record_migration(migration_path.name)
            print(f"✓ Migration {migration_path.name} completed")
            return True
            
        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
            print(f"✗ Migration failed: {e}")
            return False
    
    def _record_migration(self, name: str):
        """Record migration in tracking table."""
        # Would insert into _migrations table
        pass
    
    def migrate(self, target: str = None, dry_run: bool = False):
        """Run all pending migrations."""
        pending = self.get_pending_migrations()
        
        if not pending:
            print("✓ No pending migrations")
            return True
        
        print(f"Found {len(pending)} pending migrations")
        
        if not dry_run:
            backup = self.create_backup()
            print(f"Backup created: {backup}")
        
        for migration in pending:
            if target and migration.name > target:
                break
            
            if not self.run_migration(migration, dry_run):
                print("Migration failed. Database unchanged (within transaction).")
                return False
        
        print("\n✓ All migrations completed successfully")
        return True

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import argparse
    
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Database migration tool")
    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true", help="Show what would be executed")
    parser.add_argument("--target", help="Migrate up to specific version")
    args = parser.parse_args()
    
    migrator = DatabaseMigrator()
    success = migrator.migrate(target=args.target, dry_run=args.dry_run)
    sys.exit(0 if success else 1)
30.5 Smoke Test Automation Post-deployment verification
▶️ Comprehensive Smoke Test Script
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""smoke_test.py - Post-deployment smoke tests"""

import requests
import sys
import time
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional, Callable

@dataclass
class TestResult:
    name: str
    passed: bool
    duration_ms: float
    message: str = ""

class SmokeTestRunner:
    """Run smoke tests against deployed application."""
    
    def __init__(self, base_url: str, timeout: int = 10):
        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")
        self.timeout = timeout
        self.results: list = []
    
    def test(self, name: str, check: Callable[[], bool], description: str = "") -> TestResult:
        """Run a single test with timing."""
        start = time.time()
        try:
            passed = check()
            message = "OK" if passed else description or "Check failed"
        except Exception as e:
            passed = False
            message = str(e)
        
        duration = (time.time() - start) * 1000
        result = TestResult(name, passed, duration, message)
        self.results.append(result)
        
        status = "✓" if passed else "✗"
        print(f"  {status} {name} ({duration:.0f}ms)")
        if not passed:
            print(f"    └─ {message}")
        
        return result
    
    def check_health(self) -> bool:
        """Health endpoint responds with 200."""
        resp = requests.get(f"{self.base_url}/health", timeout=self.timeout)
        return resp.status_code == 200
    
    def check_api_response(self, endpoint: str, expected_keys: list = None) -> bool:
        """API endpoint returns valid JSON."""
        resp = requests.get(f"{self.base_url}{endpoint}", timeout=self.timeout)
        if resp.status_code != 200:
            return False
        
        data = resp.json()
        if expected_keys:
            return all(key in data for key in expected_keys)
        return True
    
    def check_response_time(self, endpoint: str, max_ms: int = 500) -> bool:
        """Endpoint responds within time limit."""
        start = time.time()
        resp = requests.get(f"{self.base_url}{endpoint}", timeout=self.timeout)
        elapsed_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000
        return resp.status_code == 200 and elapsed_ms < max_ms
    
    def check_ssl(self) -> bool:
        """SSL certificate is valid."""
        if not self.base_url.startswith("https"):
            return True
        resp = requests.get(self.base_url, timeout=self.timeout, verify=True)
        return resp.ok
    
    def run_all(self) -> bool:
        """Run all smoke tests."""
        print(f"\nRunning smoke tests against: {self.base_url}\n")
        
        # Core health checks
        self.test("Health endpoint", self.check_health)
        self.test("API responds", lambda: self.check_api_response("/api/v1/status"))
        self.test("Response time < 500ms", lambda: self.check_response_time("/api/v1/status"))
        
        # SSL/Security
        if self.base_url.startswith("https"):
            self.test("SSL certificate valid", self.check_ssl)
        
        # Business logic checks
        self.test("Users endpoint", lambda: self.check_api_response("/api/v1/users", ["data"]))
        
        # Summary
        passed = sum(1 for r in self.results if r.passed)
        total = len(self.results)
        
        print(f"\n{'='*40}")
        print(f"Results: {passed}/{total} tests passed")
        
        if passed == total:
            print("✓ All smoke tests passed!")
            return True
        else:
            print("✗ Some tests failed")
            return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import os
    
    base_url = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else os.environ.get("APP_URL", "http://localhost:8080")
    
    runner = SmokeTestRunner(base_url)
    success = runner.run_all()
    sys.exit(0 if success else 1)
30.7 Multi-Environment Deployment Environment-aware deployment
📋 Multi-Environment Architecture 
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              DEPLOYMENT PIPELINE                                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   Code ──▶ Build ──▶ Test ──▶ Deploy ──▶ Verify ──▶ Promote    │
│                              │                                   │
│                              ▼                                   │
│                      ┌──────────────┐                           │
│                      │   STAGING    │◀── Auto deploy            │
│                      │  (develop)   │                            │
│                      └──────┬───────┘                           │
│                             │ ✓ Tests pass                      │
│                             ▼                                   │
│                      ┌──────────────┐                           │
│                      │     UAT      │◀── Manual approve         │
│                      │   (release)  │                            │
│                      └──────┬───────┘                           │
│                             │ ✓ Approved                        │
│                             ▼                                   │
│                      ┌──────────────┐                           │
│                      │ PRODUCTION   │◀── Canary → Full          │
│                      │   (main)     │                            │
│                      └──────────────┘                           │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
▶️ Multi-Environment Deploy Script
#!/bin/bash
# deploy-env.sh - Environment-aware deployment

set -euo pipefail

# Configuration per environment
declare -A ENV_CONFIG
ENV_CONFIG[staging]="REPLICAS=2 RESOURCES=small DOMAIN=staging.example.com"
ENV_CONFIG[uat]="REPLICAS=2 RESOURCES=medium DOMAIN=uat.example.com"
ENV_CONFIG[production]="REPLICAS=5 RESOURCES=large DOMAIN=example.com"

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

log_info()  { echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
log_warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
log_error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; }

# Validate environment
validate_env() {
    local env="$1"
    if [[ -z "${ENV_CONFIG[$env]:-}" ]]; then
        log_error "Unknown environment: $env"
        echo "Available: ${!ENV_CONFIG[*]}"
        exit 1
    fi
}

# Load environment config
load_env_config() {
    local env="$1"
    eval "${ENV_CONFIG[$env]}"
    export ENVIRONMENT="$env"
    export KUBECONFIG="$HOME/.kube/config-$env"
}

# Pre-deployment checks
pre_deploy_checks() {
    log_info "Running pre-deployment checks..."
    
    # Check kubectl access
    if ! kubectl cluster-info > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_error "Cannot connect to Kubernetes cluster"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Check image exists
    if ! docker manifest inspect "$IMAGE" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_error "Image not found: $IMAGE"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    log_info "Pre-deployment checks passed"
}

# Deploy to environment
deploy() {
    local env="$1"
    local image="$2"
    
    validate_env "$env"
    load_env_config "$env"
    
    log_info "Deploying $image to $env"
    
    # Production requires approval
    if [[ "$env" == "production" ]]; then
        log_warn "Production deployment requested"
        read -p "Confirm deployment to PRODUCTION? [yes/NO]: " confirm
        if [[ "$confirm" != "yes" ]]; then
            log_error "Deployment cancelled"
            exit 1
        fi
    fi
    
    export IMAGE="$image"
    pre_deploy_checks
    
    # Apply Kubernetes manifests with envsubst
    envsubst < k8s/deployment.yaml | kubectl apply -f -
    envsubst < k8s/service.yaml | kubectl apply -f -
    
    # Wait for rollout
    kubectl rollout status deployment/myapp --timeout=300s
    
    # Run smoke tests
    log_info "Running smoke tests..."
    ./scripts/smoke_test.py "https://${DOMAIN}"
    
    log_info "Deployment to $env complete!"
}

# Main
case "${1:-}" in
    deploy)
        deploy "${2:-staging}" "${3:-myapp:latest}"
        ;;
    status)
        validate_env "${2:-staging}"
        load_env_config "${2:-staging}"
        kubectl get deployments,pods,svc
        ;;
    logs)
        validate_env "${2:-staging}"
        load_env_config "${2:-staging}"
        kubectl logs -l app=myapp --tail=100 -f
        ;;
    *)
        echo "Usage: $0 {deploy|status|logs} [environment] [image]"
        echo "Environments: ${!ENV_CONFIG[*]}"
        exit 1
        ;;
esac

🐳 Part 8: Container & Kubernetes Automation

Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, GitOps, and service mesh automation.

🪟 Windows Users - Docker Desktop Setup 
Docker works great on Windows! Install Docker Desktop:
1. Download: https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/
2. Install and enable WSL 2 backend (recommended)
3. All docker commands work identically in PowerShell or WSL

# Verify installation (PowerShell or WSL)
docker --version
docker run hello-world

Note: kubectl commands also work the same on Windows.
Install via: winget install Kubernetes.kubectl
